-- Hoogle documentation, generated by Haddock
-- See Hoogle, http://www.haskell.org/hoogle/


-- | Welcome to Lattest, the latest attested lattice testing tech!
--   
--   The purpose of Lattest to assist in model-based testing: set up a test
--   for external systems, where the expected behaviour of that system is
--   expressed as a specification model in the form of an automaton.
--   
--   For details on setting up such a test, see <a>Lattest.Exec.Testing</a>
@package lattest-lib
@version 0.1.0.0


-- | A simple implementation of <a>Functor</a> and <a>Monad</a> with
--   <a>Ord</a> constraints, to make operations on those data types more
--   efficient.
module Data.OrdMonad

-- | Functors with an additional <a>Ord</a> constraint. The primary use
--   case for the <a>OrdFunctor</a> is to treat data structures like
--   <tt>Set</tt> as a functor, where the <a>Ord</a>-constraint is used for
--   performance reasons. Implementations of <a>ordMap</a> should adhere to
--   the same laws as for <a>fmap</a> for a <tt><a>Functor</a> F</tt>:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><i>Identity</i> <tt><a>ordMap</a> <a>id</a> == <a>id</a></tt></li>
--   <li><i>Composition</i> <tt><a>ordMap</a> (f . g) == <a>ordMap</a> f .
--   <a>ordMap</a> g</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   The composition law is only required if the extensionality property of
--   type class <a>Eq</a> holds for the domain of <tt>f</tt>. Effectively,
--   this states that <a>Eq</a> should behave like proper equality for
--   <tt>f</tt>, or conversely, if <tt>x <a>==</a> y</tt> and <tt>f x
--   <a>/=</a> f y</tt>, then compositionality is not expected to hold.
class OrdFunctor (f :: Type -> Type)

-- | Map a function over a functorial type, just like <a>fmap</a>, but with
--   an additional <a>Ord</a> constraint.
ordMap :: (OrdFunctor f, Ord b) => (a -> b) -> f a -> f b

-- | An infix synonym for <a>ordMap</a>, similar to <a>&lt;$&gt;</a>.
(<#>) :: (OrdFunctor f, Ord b) => (a -> b) -> f a -> f b

-- | Monads with an additional <a>Ord</a> constraint. Analogously to how an
--   <a>OrdFunctor</a> specializes a regular <a>Functor</a>,
--   <a>OrdMonad</a> uses the <a>Ord</a>-constraint is used for performance
--   reasons. Any instance should adhere to the <a>Monad</a> laws, assuming
--   the extensionality property for equality <a>Eq</a>. See
--   <a>OrdFunctor</a> for details.
class OrdFunctor m => OrdMonad (m :: Type -> Type)

-- | Bind operation, using an <a>Ord</a> constraint, similar to the
--   standard monadic bind operation <a>&gt;&gt;=</a>.
ordBind :: (OrdMonad m, Ord b) => m a -> (a -> m b) -> m b

-- | Return operation, similar to the standard monadic <a>return</a>. No
--   <a>Ord</a> constraint is present here, as comparing values is not
--   needed for injecting a single value into a monadic type.
ordReturn :: OrdMonad m => a -> m a

-- | Standard monadic <tt>join</tt>, but with an additional <a>Ord</a>
--   constraint.
ordJoin :: (Ord a, OrdMonad m) => m (m a) -> m a
instance Data.OrdMonad.OrdFunctor Data.Set.Internal.Set
instance GHC.Base.Functor f => Data.OrdMonad.OrdFunctor f
instance Data.OrdMonad.OrdMonad Data.Set.Internal.Set
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => Data.OrdMonad.OrdMonad m

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Boute

-- | operator divMod on the provided integer values.
--   
--   <pre>
--   (for all ((m Int) (n Int))
--     (=&gt; (distinct n 0)
--         (let ((q (div m n)) (r (mod m n)))
--           (and (= m (+ (* n q) r))
--                (&lt;= 0 r (- (abs n) 1))))))
--   </pre>
divMod :: Integer -> Integer -> (Integer, Integer)

-- | operator divide on the provided integer values.
div :: Integer -> Integer -> Integer

-- | operator modulo on the provided integer values.
--   
--   <pre>
--   (for all ((m Int) (n Int)) 
--     (=&gt; (distinct n 0)
--                (&lt;= 0 (mod m n) (- (abs n) 1))))
--   </pre>
mod :: Integer -> Integer -> Integer


-- | <pre>
--   a0 &lt;&gt; a1 &lt;&gt; ... &lt;&gt; an-1
--   </pre>
--   
--   `ai in A`, <a>&lt;&gt;</a> is a commutative (binary) operation, `(A,
--   &lt;&gt;)` is a Monoid, there is a multiplication operator
--   <a>&lt;.&gt;</a> that is distributive w.r.t <a>&lt;&gt;</a>, and there
--   is additive inverse for the elements of <tt>A</tt>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a &lt;&gt; b         = b &lt;&gt; a                 -- for all a, b in A (commutativity)
--   a &lt;.&gt; (b &lt;&gt; c) = (a &lt;.&gt; b) &lt;&gt; (a &lt;.&gt; c) -- for all a, b, c in A (left distributivity).
--   (b &lt;&gt; c) &lt;.&gt; a = (b &lt;.&gt; a) &lt;&gt; (c &lt;.&gt; a) -- for all a, b, c in A (right distributivity).
--   a &lt;&gt; -a        = 0                      -- for all a, for some (-a), where `0 = mempty`.
--   </pre>
--   
--   Furthermore, to be able to assign semantics to this symbolic
--   representation it is required that the terms are integer-multipliable,
--   meaning that
--   
--   <pre>
--   `a &lt;.&gt; n`
--   </pre>
--   
--   is equivalent to adding an element <tt>a</tt> <tt>n</tt> times if
--   <tt>n</tt> is non-negative, or removing it <tt>n</tt> times otherwise.
--   See <a>IntMultipliable</a> class, and <tt>multiplyLaw</tt> in
--   <tt>FreeMonoidXSpec</tt>.
--   
--   Note that we're using <a>&lt;.&gt;</a> as having both types `A -&gt;
--   A` and `Integral n =&gt; n -&gt; a`, which is fine at the moment since
--   the <a>FreeMonoidX</a>'s we are dealing with are numeric types.
--   
--   `FreeMonoidX a` is an instance of the <a>IsList</a> class, which means
--   that in combination with the <tt>OverloadedLists</tt> extension is
--   possible to write free-monoids as lists. For instance the free-monoid:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a0 &lt;&gt; a1 &lt;&gt; ... &lt;&gt; an-1
--   </pre>
--   
--   Can be written as
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><i>a0, a1, ..., an-1</i></li>
--   </ul>
module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX

-- | Symbolic representation of a polynomial, where each term is a member
--   of type <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   The integer value of the map represents the number of occurrences of a
--   term in the polynomial. Given this representation it is crucial that
--   the operation is commutative, since the information of about the order
--   of the term is lost in this representation.
newtype FreeMonoidX a
FMX :: Map a Integer -> FreeMonoidX a
[asMap] :: FreeMonoidX a -> Map a Integer

-- | Number of distinct terms in the free-monoid.
nrofDistinctTerms :: FreeMonoidX a -> Int

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. The distinct terms of a free-monoid., each term occurs
--   only once in the list.
distinctTerms :: FreeMonoidX a -> [a]
distinctTermsT :: TermWrapper t => FreeMonoidX (t a) -> [a]
allFreeMonoidX :: TermWrapper f => (a -> Bool) -> FreeMonoidX (f a) -> Bool

-- | Map the terms of the free-monoid.
mapTerms :: Ord b => (a -> b) -> FreeMonoidX a -> FreeMonoidX b
mapFreeMonoidX :: (Functor f, Ord (f b)) => (a -> b) -> FreeMonoidX (f a) -> FreeMonoidX (f b)

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Partition the free-monoid into two free-monoids, one with
--   all elements that satisfy the predicate and one with all elements that
--   don't satisfy the predicate.
partition :: (a -> Bool) -> FreeMonoidX a -> (FreeMonoidX a, FreeMonoidX a)
partitionT :: TermWrapper t => (a -> Bool) -> FreeMonoidX (t a) -> (FreeMonoidX (t a), FreeMonoidX (t a))
foldrTerms :: TermWrapper f => (a -> b -> b) -> b -> FreeMonoidX (f a) -> b

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Fold over the terms of the free-monoid with their
--   multipliers.
foldOccur :: (a -> Integer -> b -> b) -> b -> FreeMonoidX a -> b

-- | Fold the free-monoid.
foldFMX :: (IntMultipliable a, Monoid a) => FreeMonoidX a -> a

-- | Fold the free-monoid of term wrappers.
fold :: (IntMultipliable (t a), Monoid (t a), TermWrapper t) => FreeMonoidX (t a) -> a

-- | Append a term to the free-monoid.
--   
--   <pre>
--   append 2 [1, 2, 3]
--   </pre>
--   
--   should be equivalent to the free-monoid.
--   
--   <pre>
--   [1, 2, 3, 2]
--   </pre>
append :: Ord a => a -> FreeMonoidX a -> FreeMonoidX a

-- | Remove a term from the free-monoid.
--   
--   <pre>
--   remove 2 (1 &lt;&gt; 2 &lt;&gt; 3)
--   </pre>
--   
--   should be equivalent to the free-monoid
--   
--   <pre>
--   (1 &lt;&gt; 3)
--   </pre>
remove :: Ord a => a -> FreeMonoidX a -> FreeMonoidX a

-- | Add the term <tt>x</tt> <tt>n</tt> times. If <tt>n</tt> is negative
--   the term will be removed <tt>n</tt> times.
--   
--   <pre>
--   appendMany 2 10 (10 &lt;&gt; 12 &lt;&gt; 12)
--   </pre>
--   
--   should be equivalent to
--   
--   (10 &lt;&gt; 10 &lt;&gt; 10 &lt;&gt; 12 &lt;&gt; 12)
--   
--   <pre>
--   appendMany (-2) 10 (10 &lt;&gt; 12 &lt;&gt; 12)
--   </pre>
--   
--   should be equivalent to
--   
--   <pre>
--   (-10 &lt;&gt; 12 &lt;&gt; 12)
--   </pre>
appendMany :: Ord a => Integer -> a -> FreeMonoidX a -> FreeMonoidX a

-- | Flatten a free-monoid.
--   
--   For instance, the monoid
--   
--   <pre>
--   (a &lt;&gt; b) &lt;&gt; (a &lt;&gt; b) &lt;&gt; a
--   </pre>
--   
--   will be rewritten as:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a &lt;&gt; a &lt;&gt; a &lt;&gt; b &lt;&gt; b
--   </pre>
--   
--   Assuming `a &lt; b`.
flatten :: Ord a => FreeMonoidX (FreeMonoidX a) -> FreeMonoidX a

-- | `n <a>.</a> x` multiplies <tt>x</tt> <tt>n</tt> times.
(<.>) :: (IntMultipliable a, Integral n) => n -> a -> a

-- | Types that can be multiplied by an integral. This restriction is
--   required to be able to assign a correct semantic to the symbolic
--   representation of the free-monoid. If the elements of the free-monoid
--   are not <a>IntMultipliable</a>, then <a>foldFMX</a> is ill-defined.
--   
--   See the test code <tt>FreeMonoidXspec</tt> for examples of instances
--   of this class.
class IntMultipliable a

-- | A term of the monoid which wraps a value. This could be for instance a
--   sum-term or a product term.
class TermWrapper (f :: Type -> Type)
wrap :: TermWrapper f => a -> f a
unwrap :: TermWrapper f => f a -> a

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Convert the free-monoid to a list of (term, occurrence)
--   tuples.
--   
--   For instance given the term:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a &lt;&gt; b &lt;&gt; a &lt;&gt; b &lt;&gt; a
--   </pre>
--   
--   <a>toOccurList</a> applied to it will result in the following list:
--   
--   <pre>
--   [(a, 3), (b, 2)]
--   </pre>
toOccurList :: FreeMonoidX a -> [(a, Integer)]

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Convert the free-monoid to a distinct ascending list of
--   term/multiplier pairs.
toDistinctAscOccurList :: FreeMonoidX a -> [(a, Integer)]

-- | <i>O(n*log n)</i>. Create a free-monoid from a list of term/multiplier
--   pairs.
fromOccurList :: Ord a => [(a, Integer)] -> FreeMonoidX a

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Build a free-monoid from an ascending list of
--   term/multiplier pairs where each term appears only once. /The
--   precondition (input list is strictly ascending) is not checked./
fromDistinctAscOccurList :: [(a, Integer)] -> FreeMonoidX a

-- | <i>O(t*log t)</i>. Create a product from a list of terms using the
--   given term wrapper.
fromListT :: (Ord (t a), TermWrapper t) => [a] -> FreeMonoidX (t a)
toOccurListT :: TermWrapper t => FreeMonoidX (t a) -> [(a, Integer)]
toDistinctAscOccurListT :: TermWrapper t => FreeMonoidX (t a) -> [(a, Integer)]
fromOccurListT :: (Ord (t a), TermWrapper t) => [(a, Integer)] -> FreeMonoidX (t a)
fromDistinctAscPowerListT :: TermWrapper t => [(a, Integer)] -> FreeMonoidX (t a)

-- | The <a>toList</a> function extracts a list of <tt>Item l</tt> from the
--   structure <tt>l</tt>. It should satisfy fromList . toList = id.
toList :: IsList l => l -> [Item l]

-- | The <a>fromList</a> function constructs the structure <tt>l</tt> from
--   the given list of <tt>Item l</tt>
fromList :: IsList l => [Item l] -> l
instance (Data.Data.Data a, GHC.Classes.Ord a) => Data.Data.Data (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.IntMultipliable (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.IsList.IsList (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Base.Monoid (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord a, GHC.Read.Read a) => GHC.Read.Read (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Base.Semigroup (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.FreeMonoidX a)

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product

-- | <a>FreeProduct</a> represents a symbolic product of terms of the type
--   parameter <tt>a</tt>. The same term can occur multiple times.
type FreeProduct a = FreeMonoidX ProductTerm a

-- | Terms of a free-monoids of the form:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a0 &lt;&gt; a1 &lt;&gt; ... &lt;&gt; an-1
--   </pre>
--   
--   where <a>&lt;&gt;</a> will be interpreted as the arithmetic
--   multiplication of terms:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a0 * a1 * ... * an-1
--   </pre>
newtype ProductTerm a
ProductTerm :: a -> ProductTerm a
[factor] :: ProductTerm a -> a

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Partition the product into the dividend and divisor.
fraction :: Ord a => FreeProduct a -> (FreeProduct a, FreeProduct a)

-- | <i>O(log n)</i>. Multiply a product with a term.
multiply :: Ord a => a -> FreeProduct a -> FreeProduct a

-- | <i>O(log n)</i>. Divide a product by a term.
divide :: Ord a => a -> FreeProduct a -> FreeProduct a

-- | <i>O(n+m)</i>. The product of two products.
product :: Ord a => FreeProduct a -> FreeProduct a -> FreeProduct a

-- | The product of a list of products.
products :: Ord a => [FreeProduct a] -> FreeProduct a

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Take the product <i>ms</i> to the power with the constant
--   <i>x</i>.
power :: Ord a => Integer -> FreeProduct a -> FreeProduct a
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm
instance Data.Data.Data a => Data.Data.Data (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Real.Integral a => Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.IntMultipliable (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Num.Num a => GHC.Base.Monoid (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Read.Read a => GHC.Read.Read (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Num.Num a => GHC.Base.Semigroup (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm a)
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.TermWrapper Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Product.ProductTerm

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum

-- | <a>FreeSum</a> represents a symbolic sum of terms of the type
--   parameter <tt>a</tt>. The same term can occur multiple times.
type FreeSum a = FreeMonoidX SumTerm a

-- | Terms of a free-monoids of the form:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a0 &lt;&gt; a1 &lt;&gt; ... &lt;&gt; an-1
--   </pre>
--   
--   where <a>&lt;&gt;</a> will be interpreted as the arithmetic sum of
--   terms:
--   
--   <pre>
--   a0 + a1 + ... + an-1
--   </pre>
newtype SumTerm a
SumTerm :: a -> SumTerm a
[summand] :: SumTerm a -> a

-- | <i>O(log n)</i>. Add a term to a sum.
add :: Ord a => a -> FreeSum a -> FreeSum a

-- | <i>O(log n)</i>. Subtract a term from a sum.
subtract :: Ord a => a -> FreeSum a -> FreeSum a

-- | <i>O(n+m)</i>. The sum of two sums.
sum :: Ord a => FreeSum a -> FreeSum a -> FreeSum a

-- | The sum of a list of sums.
sums :: Ord a => [FreeSum a] -> FreeSum a

-- | <i>O(n)</i>. Multiply the constant with the sum.
multiply :: Ord a => Integer -> FreeSum a -> FreeSum a
instance Data.Data.Data a => Data.Data.Data (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Real.Integral a => Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.IntMultipliable (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Num.Num a => GHC.Base.Monoid (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Read.Read a => GHC.Read.Read (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Num.Num a => GHC.Base.Semigroup (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm a)
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.FreeMonoidX.TermWrapper Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.Sum.SumTerm

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs
data ExprView t
[Var] :: forall t. Variable -> ExprView t
[Const] :: forall t. ExprType t => t -> ExprView t
[Ite] :: forall t. ExprView Bool -> ExprView t -> ExprView t -> ExprView t
[EqualInt] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView Bool
[EqualString] :: ExprView String -> ExprView String -> ExprView Bool
[EqualBool] :: ExprView Bool -> ExprView Bool -> ExprView Bool
[Divide] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer
[Modulo] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer
[Sum] :: FreeSum (ExprView Integer) -> ExprView Integer
[Product] :: FreeProduct (ExprView Integer) -> ExprView Integer
[Length] :: ExprView String -> ExprView Integer
[GezInt] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Bool
[Not] :: ExprView Bool -> ExprView Bool
[And] :: Set (ExprView Bool) -> ExprView Bool
[At] :: ExprView String -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView [Char]
[Concat] :: [ExprView String] -> ExprView [Char]

-- | Expr: value expression 1. User can't directly construct Expr (such
--   that invariants will always hold) 2. User can still pattern match on
--   Expr using <a>ExprView</a> 3. Overhead at run-time is zero. See
--   <a>https://wiki.haskell.org/Performance/Data_types#Newtypes</a>
newtype Expr t
Expr :: ExprView t -> Expr t
[view] :: Expr t -> ExprView t

-- | Evaluate the provided value expression. Either the Right Constant
--   Value is returned or a (Left) error message.
eval :: Expr v -> Either String v
reduce :: ExprView v -> ExprView v
data Variable
Variable :: String -> Type -> Variable
[varName] :: Variable -> String
[varType] :: Variable -> Type
data Type
IntType :: Type
BoolType :: Type
StringType :: Type
allTypes :: [Type]
data Constant

-- | Constructor of Boolean constant.
Cbool :: Bool -> Constant
[toBool] :: Constant -> Bool

-- | Constructor of Integer constant.
Cint :: Integer -> Constant
[toInteger] :: Constant -> Integer

-- | Constructor of String constant.
Cstring :: String -> Constant
[toString] :: Constant -> String

-- | Constructor of constructor constant (value of ADT).
Ccstr :: String -> [Constant] -> Constant
[cstrName] :: Constant -> String
[args] :: Constant -> [Constant]
constType :: Constant -> Type

-- | convert a Constant to an typed value
class ConstType t
fromConst :: ConstType t => Constant -> Either String t
toConst :: ConstType t => t -> Constant
class ExprType t
typeOf :: ExprType t => t -> Type
typeOf' :: ExprType t => f t -> Type
isConst :: ExprView v -> Bool
freeVars :: Expr t -> Set Variable
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ConstType GHC.Types.Bool
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ConstType GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ConstType GHC.Base.String
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Constant
instance GHC.Classes.Eq t => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Expr t)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq t => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ExprView t)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Type
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Variable
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ExprType GHC.Types.Bool
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ExprType GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ExprType GHC.Base.String
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Constant
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Constant
instance GHC.Classes.Ord t => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Expr t)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord t => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ExprView t)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Type
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Variable
instance GHC.Read.Read Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Constant
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Constant
instance GHC.Show.Show t => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Expr t)
instance GHC.Show.Show t => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.ExprView t)
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Type
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Variable
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Constant

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls

-- | Create a function call. Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
--   
--   Is Expr a Constant/Value Expression? isConst :: Expr -&gt; Bool
--   isConst (view -&gt; Vconst{}) = True isConst _ = False
sConst :: ExprType t => t -> Expr t
sTrue :: Expr Bool
sFalse :: Expr Bool
sVar :: VarExpr t => Variable -> Expr t
(.==) :: EqExpr t => Expr t -> Expr t -> Expr Bool
infix 4 .==

-- | Apply operator ITE (IF THEN ELSE) on the provided value expressions.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sIfThenElse :: Expr Bool -> Expr t -> Expr t -> Expr t

-- | Apply operator Not on the provided value expression. Preconditions are
--   <i>not</i> checked.
sNot :: Expr Bool -> Expr Bool

-- | Apply operator And on the provided set of value expressions.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sAnd :: Set (Expr Bool) -> Expr Bool
sSum :: FreeSum (Expr Integer) -> Expr Integer
sProduct :: FreeProduct (Expr Integer) -> Expr Integer

-- | Apply operator Divide on the provided value expressions. Preconditions
--   are <i>not</i> checked.
(./) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Integer
infixl 7 ./

-- | Apply operator Modulo on the provided value expressions. Preconditions
--   are <i>not</i> checked.
(.%) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Integer
infixl 7 .%

-- | Apply operator GEZ (Greater Equal Zero) on the provided value
--   expression. Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sIsNonNegative :: Expr Integer -> Expr Bool

-- | Apply operator Length on the provided value expression. Preconditions
--   are <i>not</i> checked.
sLength :: Expr String -> Expr Integer

-- | Apply operator At on the provided value expressions. Preconditions are
--   <i>not</i> checked.
(.@) :: Expr String -> Expr Integer -> Expr String
infixl 5 .@

-- | Apply operator Concat on the provided sequence of value expressions.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sConcat :: [Expr String] -> Expr String
data VarModel
class Assignable t
assign :: Assignable t => Variable -> Expr t -> VarModel -> VarModel
data Valuation
toConstantsMap :: Valuation -> Map Variable Constant
fromConstantsMap :: Map Variable Constant -> Valuation
emptyValuation :: Valuation
assignValues :: [Valuation -> Valuation] -> Valuation
assignValue :: Assignable t => Variable -> t -> Valuation -> Valuation
insertIntoValuation :: Variable -> Constant -> Valuation -> Valuation
substConst :: Assignable t => Valuation -> Expr t -> Expr t

-- | Substitute variables by value expressions in a value expression.
--   
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
subst :: Assignable t => VarModel -> Expr t -> Expr t
assignedExpr :: Assignable t => Variable -> VarModel -> Maybe (Expr t)
assignment :: [VarModel -> VarModel] -> VarModel
noAssignment :: VarModel
(=:) :: Assignable t => Variable -> Expr t -> VarModel -> VarModel
infixr 0 =:
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.Assignable GHC.Types.Bool
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.Assignable GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.Assignable GHC.Base.String
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.EqExpr GHC.Types.Bool
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.EqExpr GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.EqExpr GHC.Base.String
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.Valuation
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.VarModel
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.Valuation
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.VarModel
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.Valuation
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.VarModel
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.VarExpr GHC.Types.Bool
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.VarExpr GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImpls.VarExpr GHC.Base.String

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprImplsExtension

-- | Apply operator Or (\/) on the provided set of value expressions.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sOr :: Set (Expr Bool) -> Expr Bool
(.&&) :: Expr Bool -> Expr Bool -> Expr Bool
infixr 3 .&&
(.||) :: Expr Bool -> Expr Bool -> Expr Bool
infixr 2 .||

-- | Apply operator Xor (\|/) on the provided set of value expressions.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sXor :: Expr Bool -> Expr Bool -> Expr Bool

-- | Apply operator Implies (=&gt;) on the provided value expressions.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
(.=>) :: Expr Bool -> Expr Bool -> Expr Bool
infixr 1 .=>

-- | Apply unary operator Minus on the provided value expression.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sNeg :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer

-- | Apply operator Add on the provided value expressions. Preconditions
--   are <i>not</i> checked.
(.+) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Integer
infixl 6 .+

-- | Apply operator Minus on the provided value expressions. Preconditions
--   are <i>not</i> checked.
(.-) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Integer
infixl 6 .-

-- | Apply operator Times on the provided value expressions. Preconditions
--   are <i>not</i> checked.
(.*) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Integer
infixl 7 .*

-- | Apply operator Absolute value (abs) on the provided value expression.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
sAbs :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer

-- | Apply operator LT (&lt;) on the provided value expression.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
(.<) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Bool
infix 4 .<

-- | Apply operator LE (&lt;=) on the provided value expression.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
(.<=) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Bool
infix 4 .<=

-- | Apply operator GE (&gt;=) on the provided value expression.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
(.>=) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Bool
infix 4 .>=

-- | Apply operator GT (&gt;) on the provided value expression.
--   Preconditions are <i>not</i> checked.
(.>) :: Expr Integer -> Expr Integer -> Expr Bool
infix 4 .>
instance GHC.Num.Num (Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Internal.ExprDefs.Expr GHC.Num.Integer.Integer)

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.Expr

-- | Expr: value expression 1. User can't directly construct Expr (such
--   that invariants will always hold) 2. User can still pattern match on
--   Expr using <a>ExprView</a> 3. Overhead at run-time is zero. See
--   <a>https://wiki.haskell.org/Performance/Data_types#Newtypes</a>
data Expr t
view :: Expr t -> ExprView t
data ExprView t
[Var] :: forall t. Variable -> ExprView t
[Const] :: forall t. ExprType t => t -> ExprView t
[Ite] :: forall t. ExprView Bool -> ExprView t -> ExprView t -> ExprView t
[EqualInt] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView Bool
[EqualString] :: ExprView String -> ExprView String -> ExprView Bool
[EqualBool] :: ExprView Bool -> ExprView Bool -> ExprView Bool
[Divide] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer
[Modulo] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView Integer
[Sum] :: FreeSum (ExprView Integer) -> ExprView Integer
[Product] :: FreeProduct (ExprView Integer) -> ExprView Integer
[Length] :: ExprView String -> ExprView Integer
[GezInt] :: ExprView Integer -> ExprView Bool
[Not] :: ExprView Bool -> ExprView Bool
[And] :: Set (ExprView Bool) -> ExprView Bool
[At] :: ExprView String -> ExprView Integer -> ExprView [Char]
[Concat] :: [ExprView String] -> ExprView [Char]
data Type
IntType :: Type
BoolType :: Type
StringType :: Type
data Constant

-- | Constructor of Boolean constant.
Cbool :: Bool -> Constant
[toBool] :: Constant -> Bool

-- | Constructor of Integer constant.
Cint :: Integer -> Constant
[toInteger] :: Constant -> Integer

-- | Constructor of String constant.
Cstring :: String -> Constant
[toString] :: Constant -> String

-- | Constructor of constructor constant (value of ADT).
Ccstr :: String -> [Constant] -> Constant
[cstrName] :: Constant -> String
[args] :: Constant -> [Constant]
constType :: Constant -> Type

-- | convert a Constant to an typed value
class ConstType t
fromConst :: ConstType t => Constant -> Either String t
toConst :: ConstType t => t -> Constant
data Variable
Variable :: String -> Type -> Variable
[varName] :: Variable -> String
[varType] :: Variable -> Type

-- | Evaluate the provided value expression. Either the Right Constant
--   Value is returned or a (Left) error message.
eval :: Expr v -> Either String v
freeVars :: Expr t -> Set Variable


-- | A <i>bounded monad</i> is a type constructor which represents the
--   observable perspective on the state of an automaton, also called a
--   <i>state configuration</i>. A system will internally have a state, but
--   this state can not always be observed or inferred for an external
--   viewer. To model the difference between internal and observable state,
--   we define automata as having a type of internal state, say q, and an
--   observable state configuration over q.
--   
--   In this module, we define three such state configurations:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>deterministic state configurations, where every behaviour leads to
--   a single state,</li>
--   <li>non-deterministic state configurations, where given observable
--   behaviour may lead to a set of states, and</li>
--   <li>distributive lattices, or positive boolean formulas, where the
--   observable behaviour is expressed as a logical expression over
--   states.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Here, 'observed behaviour' is formally a trace or sequence of
--   observable actions. Thus, after a trace, the system is in a state
--   configuration over states. The state configuration is computed by
--   taking transitions, following the monadic interpret of the state
--   configuration. If the automaton does not exhibit a given trace of
--   actions, then the state configuration after that trace is either
--   <a>forbidden</a> or <a>underspecified</a>. Here, <a>forbidden</a>
--   expresses that the automaton does not allow the given trace, whereas
--   <a>underspecified</a> expresses that the automaton does not specify
--   the trace, hence the trace is allowed.
--   
--   The name 'bounded monad' is based on bounded lattices, which have a
--   top and bottom element. For more details on the use of top and bottom
--   in state configurations, see
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a><i>Ramon Janssen</i>, Refinement and partiality for model-based
--   testing (Doctoral dissertation), 2022, Chapter 4</a></li>
--   </ul>
module Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad

-- | Deterministic state configuration. This means that an automaton is
--   either in a single state, or in an explicit forbidden configuration,
--   or in an explicit underspecified configuration.
data Det q
Det :: q -> Det q
ForbiddenDet :: Det q
UnderspecDet :: Det q

-- | Non-deterministic state configuration. This means that an automaton
--   non-deterministically in a number of states, where zero states
--   indicates the forbidden configuration, or in an explicit
--   underspecified configuration.
data NonDet q
NonDet :: Set q -> NonDet q
UnderspecNonDet :: NonDet q
nonDet :: Ord q => [q] -> NonDet q

-- | Free distributive lattice, or a positive boolean formula, i.e., a
--   boolean formula with conjunctions and disjunctions over atomic
--   propositions. The two elements <a>top</a> and <a>bot</a> can be
--   interpreted as true and false.
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: this implementation is functionally correct, but not
--   very efficient when repeatedly applying operators, especially
--   <a>fmap</a> and monadic bind <a>&gt;&gt;=</a>, since no reductions are
--   performed.
data FreeLattice a

-- | A single state embedded in a free distributive lattice.
atom :: a -> FreeLatticeCNF a

-- | The free distributive lattice element ⊤, or true.
top :: FreeLatticeCNF a

-- | The free distributive lattice element ⊥, or false.
bot :: FreeLatticeCNF a

-- | Free distributive lattice, or a positive boolean formula, in
--   CNF-format. Behaviourally, this is equivalent to the standard
--   <a>FreeLattice</a>, but the size is bounded by the normal form. This
--   makes it potentially more efficient when repeatedly applying
--   operators, especially <a>fmap</a> and monadic bind <a>&gt;&gt;=</a>,
--   but also potentially slightly <i>less</i> efficient for small
--   lattices.
newtype FreeLatticeCNF a
FreeLatticeCNF :: Set (Set a) -> FreeLatticeCNF a

-- | Specifiednesss describe wether behaviour (a sequence of actions) is
--   allowed a stateful specification model. <a>Forbidden</a> describes
--   that behaviour is not allowed, not is any subsequent behaviour.
--   <a>Underspecified</a> describes that behaviour is allowed and any
--   subsequent behaviour is also allowed. <a>Indefinite</a> means that the
--   behaviour is allowed, and subsequent behaviour may be forbidden,
--   underspecified, or may again be <a>Indefinite</a>.
data Specifiedness
Underspecified :: Specifiedness
Forbidden :: Specifiedness
Indefinite :: Specifiedness

-- | Specifiedness configurations are state configurations which have a
--   representation for forbidden and underspecified configurations.
class BoundedConfiguration (m :: Type -> Type)

-- | Extract the current <a>Specifiedness</a> from a bounded monad.
specifiedness :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Specifiedness
forbidden :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t
underspecified :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t
isForbidden :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Bool
isUnderspecified :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Bool

-- | Is the configuration a representation of "allowed", i.e.,
--   <a>Indefinite</a> or <a>Underspecified</a>?
isAllowed :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Bool

-- | Is the configuration a representation of "specified", i.e.,
--   <a>Indefinite</a> or <a>Forbidden</a>?
isSpecified :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Bool

-- | Is the configuration a representation of the <a>Indefinite</a>
--   specifiedness?
isIndefinite :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Bool

-- | Is the configuration a representation of "definitive", i.e.,
--   <a>Forbidden</a> or <a>Underspecified</a>?
isConclusive :: BoundedConfiguration m => m t -> Bool

-- | Abbreviation for types which are both bounded configurations and
--   Monads.
type BoundedMonad (m :: Type -> Type) = (BoundedConfiguration m, OrdMonad m)

-- | Abbreviation for types which are both bounded configurations and
--   Functors.
type BoundedFunctor (m :: Type -> Type) = (BoundedConfiguration m, OrdFunctor m)

-- | Semi-lattices with a binary join (<a>or</a>) operation
class JoinSemiLattice a

-- | Semi-lattices with a binary meet (<a>and</a>) operation
class MeetSemiLattice a
(\/) :: JoinSemiLattice a => a -> a -> a
(/\) :: MeetSemiLattice a => a -> a -> a
class BooleanConfiguration (m :: Type -> Type)
asExpr :: BooleanConfiguration m => m (Expr Bool) -> Expr Bool
asDualExpr :: (OrdFunctor m, BooleanConfiguration m) => m (Expr Bool) -> Expr Bool
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BooleanConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BooleanConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BooleanConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BooleanConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BoundedConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BoundedConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BoundedConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BoundedConfiguration Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet
instance GHC.Classes.Eq q => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det q)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Specifiedness
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.JoinSemiLattice (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.JoinSemiLattice (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.JoinSemiLattice (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet a)
instance Algebra.Lattice.Lattice (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice a)
instance Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.MeetSemiLattice (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.MeetSemiLattice (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF a)
instance GHC.Base.Monad Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det
instance GHC.Base.Monad Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice
instance Data.OrdMonad.OrdFunctor Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF
instance Data.OrdMonad.OrdFunctor Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet
instance Data.OrdMonad.OrdMonad Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF
instance Data.OrdMonad.OrdMonad Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet
instance GHC.Classes.Ord q => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det q)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Algebra.Lattice.Free.Free a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Specifiedness
instance GHC.Read.Read Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Specifiedness
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Det a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLattice a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.FreeLatticeCNF a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.NonDet a)
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.Specifiedness


-- | This module contains the definitions and semantics of different forms
--   of observable actions, and inputs used in testing experiments.
module Lattest.Model.Alphabet

-- | If an input type is an <a>TestChoice</a> to a type of observable
--   actions, this means that
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>given such an input, it is possible to derive the corresponding
--   observable action, or sequence of actions, and</li>
--   <li>an observable action may (but also may not) correspond to an
--   input.</li>
--   </ul>
class TestChoice i act

-- | Derive the sequence of observable actions that correspond to an input.
choiceToActs :: TestChoice i act => i -> [act]

-- | If an observable action corresponds to an input, then derive that
--   input.
actToChoice :: TestChoice i act => act -> Maybe i

-- | Observable actions that may be either inputs provided to a system, or
--   outputs from that system.
data IOAct i o
In :: i -> IOAct i o
Out :: o -> IOAct i o

-- | Is the given action an input?
isInput :: IOAct i o -> Bool

-- | Is the given action an output?
isOutput :: IOAct i o -> Bool

-- | Partially defined function that unpacks an input.
fromInput :: IOAct i o -> i

-- | Unpacks an input.
maybeFromInput :: IOAct i o -> Maybe i

-- | Partially defined function that unpacks an outputs.
fromOutput :: IOAct i o -> o

-- | Unpacks an output.
maybeFromOutput :: IOAct i o -> Maybe o

-- | Add observation of quiescence to a type of observable actions.
data Suspended o
Quiescence :: Suspended o
OutSusp :: o -> Suspended o
δ :: IOAct i (Suspended o)

-- | Add observation of quiescence to the observed inputs and outputs.
type IOSuspAct i o = IOAct i Suspended o

-- | Convert an input or output to a type containing quiescence.
asSuspended :: IOAct i o -> IOSuspAct i o

-- | Partially defined function that unpacks an input or output from a type
--   with quiescence.
fromSuspended :: IOSuspAct i o -> IOAct i o

-- | Observable actions that may be either inputs provided to a system, or
--   outputs from that system, where the <tt>inputs</tt> may be refused.
type IFAct i o = IOAct InputAttempt i o
newtype InputAttempt i
InputAttempt :: (i, Bool) -> InputAttempt i

-- | Convert an input or output to a type containing input failures.
asInputAttempt :: IOAct i o -> IFAct i o

-- | Partially defined function that unpacks an input or output from a type
--   with input failures.
fromInputAttempt :: IFAct i o -> IOAct i o

-- | Input failure with observed quiescence. See <a>IOSuspAct</a> and
--   <a>IFAct</a> for details.
type SuspendedIF i o = IOAct InputAttempt i Suspended o

-- | Convert an input or output to a type containing input failures and
--   quiescence.
asSuspendedInputAttempt :: IOAct i o -> SuspendedIF i o

-- | Partially defined function that unpacks an input or output from a type
--   with input failures and quiescence.
fromSuspendedInputAttempt :: SuspendedIF i o -> IOAct i o
data SymInteract g
SymInteract :: g -> [Variable] -> SymInteract g
type IOSymInteract i o = SymInteract IOAct i o
type SymGuard = Expr Bool
data GateValue g
GateValue :: g -> [Constant] -> GateValue g
[gate] :: GateValue g -> g
[values] :: GateValue g -> [Constant]
type IOGateValue i o = GateValue IOAct i o
gateValueAsIOAct :: IOGateValue i o -> IOAct (GateValue i) (GateValue o)
ioActAsGateValue :: IOAct (GateValue i) (GateValue o) -> IOGateValue i o
maybeFromInputInteraction :: IOSymInteract i o -> Maybe (SymInteract i)
maybeFromOutputInteraction :: IOSymInteract i o -> Maybe (SymInteract o)
isInputGate :: IOGateValue i o -> Bool
isOutputGate :: IOGateValue i o -> Bool
isInputInteract :: IOSymInteract i o -> Bool
isOutputInteract :: IOSymInteract i o -> Bool
interactionGate :: SymInteract g -> g
valueGate :: GateValue g -> g
type IOSuspGateValue i o = IOGateValue i Suspended o
type IFGateValue i o = IOGateValue InputAttempt i o
type SuspendedIFGateValue i o = IOGateValue InputAttempt i Suspended o
toIOGateValue :: SuspendedIF (GateValue i) (GateValue o) -> SuspendedIFGateValue i o
instance GHC.Classes.Eq g => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g)
instance (GHC.Classes.Eq i, GHC.Classes.Eq o) => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq i => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.InputAttempt i)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq o => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.Suspended o)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq g => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract g)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON a => Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue a)
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord g => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord i => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.InputAttempt i)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord o => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.Suspended o)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord g => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract g)
instance GHC.Show.Show g => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g)
instance (GHC.Show.Show i, GHC.Show.Show o) => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o)
instance GHC.Show.Show i => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.InputAttempt i)
instance GHC.Show.Show o => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.Suspended o)
instance GHC.Show.Show g => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract g)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue i) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IFGateValue i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue i) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOGateValue i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice (GHC.Maybe.Maybe (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue i)) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SuspendedIFGateValue i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice (GHC.Maybe.Maybe (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue i)) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSuspGateValue i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice (GHC.Maybe.Maybe i) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SuspendedIF i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice (GHC.Maybe.Maybe i) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSuspAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice i (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IFAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Alphabet.TestChoice i (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON a => Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue a)

module Lattest.SMT.ParamCore
type Params = Map String (String, String -> Bool)
initParams :: Params


-- | This module defines the options that can be passed to TorXakis.
module Lattest.SMT.Config

-- | TorXakis configuration options.
data Config
Config :: Bool -> Map SolverId SolverConfig -> SolverId -> [(ParamName, ParamValue)] -> Config

-- | Log all SMT commands?
[smtLog] :: Config -> Bool

-- | Available solvers that can be chosen from.
[availableSolvers] :: Config -> Map SolverId SolverConfig
[selectedSolver] :: Config -> SolverId
[configuredParameters] :: Config -> [(ParamName, ParamValue)]

-- | Return a <a>CreateProcess</a> according to the selected solver in the
--   configuration.
getProc :: Config -> Maybe CreateProcess

-- | TorXakis default configuration
defaultConfig :: Config
newtype SolverId
SolverId :: String -> SolverId
[solverId] :: SolverId -> String
data SolverConfig
SolverConfig :: FilePath -> [String] -> SolverConfig

-- | Executable name.
[execName] :: SolverConfig -> FilePath

-- | Arguments for to be passed to the smtSolver.
[smtArgs] :: SolverConfig -> [String]
newtype ParamName
ParamName :: String -> ParamName
newtype ParamValue
ParamValue :: String -> ParamValue
changeSolver :: Config -> String -> Config
changeLog :: Config -> Bool -> Config
addSolvers :: Config -> Map SolverId SolverConfig -> Config
setParams :: Config -> [(ParamName, ParamValue)] -> Config

-- | Updates a Map of parameters' values with given list of configured
--   parameter
updateParamVals :: Params -> [(ParamName, ParamValue)] -> Params
updateCfg :: Maybe a -> (Config -> a -> Config) -> Config -> Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.Config.Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.Config.ParamName
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.Config.ParamValue
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.Config.SolverConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.Config.SolverId
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.SMT.Config.ParamName
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.SMT.Config.ParamValue
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.SMT.Config.SolverId
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.Config.Config
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.Config.ParamName
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.Config.ParamValue
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.Config.SolverConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.Config.SolverId

module Lattest.SMT.SMT2TXS

module Lattest.SMT.SMTAlex

-- | Data structure for Smt Tokens.
data Token
Tname :: AlexPosn -> String -> Token
Tbool :: AlexPosn -> Bool -> Token
Tinteger :: AlexPosn -> Integer -> Token
Tstring :: AlexPosn -> String -> Token
Tlet :: AlexPosn -> Token
Topenpar :: AlexPosn -> Token
Tclosepar :: AlexPosn -> Token
Tminus :: AlexPosn -> Token

-- | Smt lexer.
smtLexer :: String -> [Token]
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.SMTAlex.AlexPosn
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.SMTAlex.Token
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.SMT.SMTAlex.AlexPosn
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.SMTAlex.AlexPosn
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.SMTAlex.Token

module Lattest.SMT.SMTData
type SMT a = StateT SmtEnv IO a
data SmtEnv
SmtEnv :: Handle -> Handle -> ProcessHandle -> Maybe Handle -> SmtEnv
[inHandle] :: SmtEnv -> Handle
[outHandle] :: SmtEnv -> Handle
[smtProcessHandle] :: SmtEnv -> ProcessHandle
[logFileHandle] :: SmtEnv -> Maybe Handle
SmtEnvError :: SmtEnv
type SMTRef = IORef SmtEnv
newSMTRef :: SmtEnv -> IO SMTRef
runSMT :: SMTRef -> SMT a -> IO a
readSMTRef :: SMTRef -> IO SmtEnv

module Lattest.SMT.SMTStringAlex

-- | Data structure for Smt String Tokens.
data Token
Tquotes :: AlexPosn -> Token
TescSequence :: AlexPosn -> String -> Token
Tchar :: AlexPosn -> String -> Token

-- | Smt String lexer.
smtStringLexer :: String -> [Token]
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.SMTStringAlex.AlexPosn
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.SMTStringAlex.Token
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.SMT.SMTStringAlex.AlexPosn
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.SMTStringAlex.AlexPosn
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.SMTStringAlex.Token

module Lattest.SMT.SMTStringHappy

-- | Smt String Parser.
smtStringParser :: [Token] -> String
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Lattest.SMT.SMTStringHappy.HappyIdentity
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.SMT.SMTStringHappy.HappyIdentity
instance GHC.Base.Monad Lattest.SMT.SMTStringHappy.HappyIdentity

module Lattest.SMT.SMTString

-- | Encode String to SMT.
--   
--   According to smt-lib-version 2.5 standard
--   (http:/<i>smtlib.cs.uiowa.edu</i>papers/smt-lib-reference-v2.5-r2015-06-28.pdf),
--   quote and escape characters are escaped.
--   
--   Furthermore, prevent CVC4 Parse Error "Extended/unprintable characters
--   are not part of SMT-LIB, and they must be encoded as escape sequences"
stringToSMT :: Text -> Text

-- | Decode SMT to String.
stringFromSMT :: Text -> Text


-- | Parse SMT response.
module Lattest.SMT.SMTHappy

-- | Smt Parser.
smtParser :: [Token] -> Map String Constant
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Lattest.SMT.SMTHappy.HappyIdentity
instance GHC.Base.Functor Lattest.SMT.SMTHappy.HappyIdentity
instance GHC.Base.Monad Lattest.SMT.SMTHappy.HappyIdentity

module Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs
type Solution v = Map v Constant
data SolvableProblem
Sat :: SolvableProblem
Unsat :: SolvableProblem
Unknown :: SolvableProblem
data SolveProblem v
Solved :: Solution v -> SolveProblem v
Unsolvable :: SolveProblem v
UnableToSolve :: SolveProblem v
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolvableProblem
instance GHC.Classes.Eq v => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolveProblem v)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolvableProblem
instance GHC.Classes.Ord v => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolveProblem v)
instance GHC.Read.Read Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolvableProblem
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord v, GHC.Read.Read v) => GHC.Read.Read (Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolveProblem v)
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolvableProblem
instance GHC.Show.Show v => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.SMT.SolveDefs.SolveProblem v)

module Lattest.SMT.TXS2SMT

-- | convert sort definitions to SMT type declarations (as multiple lines
--   of commands)
--   
--   Convert function definitions to SMT type declarations (as multiple
--   lines of commands).
assertionsToSMT :: [Expr Bool] -> Text
declarationsToSMT :: [Variable] -> Text
class ConstToSMT t
valexprToSMT :: ConstToSMT t => Expr t -> Text
instance Lattest.SMT.TXS2SMT.ConstToSMT GHC.Types.Bool
instance Lattest.SMT.TXS2SMT.ConstToSMT GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Lattest.SMT.TXS2SMT.ConstToSMT GHC.Base.String

module Lattest.SMT.SMTInternal
openSolver :: SMT String
close :: SMT ()
push :: SMT ()
pop :: SMT ()
createSMTRef :: CreateProcess -> Bool -> IO SMTRef
createSMTEnv :: CreateProcess -> Bool -> IO SmtEnv
addDeclarations :: [Variable] -> SMT ()
addAssertions :: [Expr Bool] -> SMT ()
getSolvable :: SMT SolvableProblem
getSolution :: [Variable] -> SMT Valuation
getInfo :: String -> SMT String
init :: SMT ()

-- | execute the SMT command given as a string
put :: String -> SMT ()
putT :: Text -> SMT ()

-- | Transform value expression to an SMT string.
valExprToString :: ConstToSMT t => Expr t -> SMT Text
showErrors :: Handle -> String -> IO ()
getResponse :: Handle -> Integer -> IO String
getSMTresponse :: SMT String
countBracket :: String -> Integer
skipCountInsideString :: String -> Integer

module Lattest.SMT.SMT
createSMTEnv :: CreateProcess -> Bool -> IO SmtEnv
openSolver :: SMT String
close :: SMT ()
addDeclarations :: [Variable] -> SMT ()
addAssertions :: [Expr Bool] -> SMT ()
getSolvable :: SMT SolvableProblem
getSolution :: [Variable] -> SMT Valuation
push :: SMT ()
pop :: SMT ()

-- | execute the SMT command given as a string
put :: String -> SMT ()
putT :: Text -> SMT ()

-- | Transform value expression to an SMT string.
valExprToString :: ConstToSMT t => Expr t -> SMT Text
type SMTRef = IORef SmtEnv
createSMTRef :: CreateProcess -> Bool -> IO SMTRef
newSMTRef :: SmtEnv -> IO SMTRef
runSMT :: SMTRef -> SMT a -> IO a
readSMTRef :: SMTRef -> IO SmtEnv
type Solution v = Map v Constant
data SolvableProblem
Sat :: SolvableProblem
Unsat :: SolvableProblem
Unknown :: SolvableProblem
type SMT a = StateT SmtEnv IO a

module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.SolveSymPrim

-- | Combine the given guards into one.
combineGuards :: (BooleanConfiguration m, OrdFunctor m) => m SymGuard -> SymGuard

-- | In the given guard, substitute the given valuation.
substituteInGuard :: Valuation -> SymGuard -> SymGuard

-- | Evaluate the given guard
evaluateGuard :: SymGuard -> Bool

-- | For the given list of interactions and guards, using SMT solving, pick
--   the first interaction in that list for which the guard is satisfiable,
--   if any. The returned gate values for that interaction are not
--   randomized in any way, picking values is left to the SMT solver.
solveAnySequential :: [(SymInteract g, SymGuard)] -> SMT (Maybe (GateValue g))
solveGuard :: [Variable] -> SymGuard -> SMT (Maybe Valuation)


-- | An unsorted collection of utility functions for working with the IO
--   monad.
module Lattest.Util.IOUtils

-- | If-statement for monads, with a pure condition: if the condition
--   holds, execute the given monad and discard the result, otherwise
--   return ().
ifM_ :: Applicative m => Bool -> m a -> m ()

-- | If-statement for monads, with a monadic condition: if the condition
--   holds, execute the given monad and discard the result, otherwise
--   return ().
ifMM_ :: Monad m => m Bool -> m a -> m ()

-- | If-statement for monads, with a pure condition: if the condition
--   holds, execute the given monad and return the result, otherwise return
--   Nothing.
ifM :: Applicative m => Bool -> m a -> m (Maybe a)

-- | If-statement for monads, with a monadic condition: if the condition
--   holds, execute the given monad and return the result, otherwise return
--   Nothing.
ifMM :: Monad m => m Bool -> m a -> m (Maybe a)

-- | While-loop for monads. Any state passed between loop iterations should
--   be carried by the monad.
whileM :: Monad m => m Bool -> m () -> m ()

-- | While-loop for monads with explicit state passing.
transformWhile :: Monad m => a -> (a -> m (a, Bool)) -> m a

-- | Execute the given Monad, or return () if not present.
doMaybe :: Applicative m => Maybe (m ()) -> m ()

-- | An STM action that waits for the given STM condition
waitUntil :: STM Bool -> STM ()

-- | An STM action that waits for the given STM condition, and then
--   performs the given STM action
doAfter :: STM Bool -> STM a -> STM a

-- | Produce an IO monad that behaves like the given Mealy machine, with
--   separate transition and output functions.
statefulIO :: (q -> i -> q) -> (q -> i -> o) -> q -> IO (i -> IO o)

-- | Produce an IO monad that behaves like the given Mealy machine, with a
--   combined transition/output function.
statefulIO' :: (q -> i -> (q, o)) -> q -> IO (i -> IO o)

module Lattest.Util.ReportUtils

-- | Append a list of <a>TestResult</a> rows to a CSV file, with optional
--   buffering given by <tt>threshold</tt>. - If <tt>threshold</tt> is 0,
--   results are written immediately. - If <tt>threshold</tt> &gt; 0,
--   results are buffered until the buffer length reaches the threshold, at
--   which point they are written to the file. Returns the updated
--   (reverseBuffer, bufferLength) pair. Note: this function assumes the
--   CSV file already exists.
writeResults :: FilePath -> [TestResult] -> Seq TestResult -> Int -> IO (Seq TestResult)

-- | Flush remaining <a>TestResult</a> rows to a CSV file. Note: this
--   function assumes the CSV file already exists.
flushResults :: FilePath -> Seq TestResult -> IO ()

-- | Initialize a CSV file for test results with an optional header. If no
--   header is provided, a default one will be used.
initResultsFile :: FilePath -> Maybe (String, String, String) -> IO ()
data TestResult
TestResult :: Int -> String -> String -> TestResult
[testNumber] :: TestResult -> Int
[verdict] :: TestResult -> String
[trace] :: TestResult -> String
instance Data.Csv.Conversion.ToRecord Lattest.Util.ReportUtils.TestResult


-- | An unsorted collection of utility functions.
module Lattest.Util.Utils

-- | Disjunction lifted to functions.
(|||) :: (a -> Bool) -> (a -> Bool) -> a -> Bool
infixr 2 |||

-- | Conjunction lifted to functions.
(&&&) :: (a -> Bool) -> (a -> Bool) -> a -> Bool
infixr 3 &&&

-- | Disjunction lifted to a doubly nested monad.
(||^^) :: (Monad m1, Monad m2) => m1 (m2 Bool) -> m1 (m2 Bool) -> m1 (m2 Bool)
infixr 3 ||^^

-- | Conjunction lifted to a doubly nested monad.
(&&^^) :: (Monad m1, Monad m2) => m1 (m2 Bool) -> m1 (m2 Bool) -> m1 (m2 Bool)
infixr 3 &&^^

-- | Flip a coin for which the chance of result <a>True</a> is p, where p
--   is clamped to [0,1].
flipCoin :: RandomGen g => g -> Double -> (Bool, g)

-- | Take a random element from the list. Gives an error for the empty
--   list, so the user should check that first.
takeRandom :: RandomGen g => g -> [a] -> (a, g)

-- | Filter out Nothings from a Foldable of Maybes, and returns only the
--   Justs.
takeJusts :: Foldable f => f (Maybe t) -> [t]

-- | If the first tuple element is a Nothing, then take a Nothing, ignoring
--   the second element, otherwise take a Just of both elements.
distributeFirstMaybe :: (Maybe a, b) -> Maybe (a, b)

-- | Remove an arbitrary element from the given set, and return both that
--   element and the remaining set, or <a>Nothing</a> if the given set was
--   empty.
takeArbitrary :: Set a -> Maybe (a, Set a)

-- | Remove duplicates from a list by sorting, grouping and taking only the
--   first element of each group. Returns ordered and filtered list.
removeDuplicates :: Ord a => [a] -> [a]

-- | Apply a monadic computation over a functor, where the monadic
--   computations are performed sequentially according to the natural
--   ordering of the functor elements. If m is the <tt>Identity</tt> monad,
--   then this function boils down to a normal <a>fmap</a>.
--   
--   Strictly speaking, this function is partial: it is undefined if the
--   functor maps the computation over an element which is not folded by
--   that same functor. In practice, the elements mapped over by a foldable
--   functor are usually exactly the folded elements.
distributeMonadOverFoldable :: (OrdFunctor f, Foldable f, Monad m, Ord x, Ord y) => (x -> m y) -> f x -> m (f y)

module Lattest.Util.ModelParsingUtils

-- | Read an .aut file representing an LTS from the provided filepath,
--   parse its content and return: - [String]: Input Alphabet - [String]:
--   Output Alphabet - Set.Set String: Set of all LTS states - String:
--   Initial state - Maybe [(String, IOAct String String, String)]: List of
--   LTS transition tuples as (InitialState, Action, EndState) NOTE: In
--   order to parse actions correctly, inputs and outputs must end in _i
--   and _o respectively. The first line of the .aut file must follow the
--   structure des (initState,nEdges,nStates).
readAutFile :: FilePath -> IO ([String], [String], Set String, String, Maybe [(String, IOAct String String, String)])

-- | Build a .dot file representation of LTS transitions and save it in the
--   specified File Path
dumpLTSdot :: (Show s, Show i, Show o) => FilePath -> [(s, IOAct i o, s)] -> IO ()


-- | This module contains the definitions and interpretations of automata
--   models.
module Lattest.Model.Automaton

-- | Syntactical automaton model, with locations and transitions. This is
--   analogous to an automaton drawn on paper with points and arrows.
--   Transitions are mapped to <i>state configurations</i>, see
--   <a>Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad</a>. Furthermore, transitions contain
--   transition labels, both on the <tt>outside</tt> and in the
--   <tt>inside</tt> of the state configuration.
--   
--   These labels are abstract and may be interpreted in various ways, e.g.
--   a simple automaton model may directly have observable actions as
--   labels, whereas a more complex automaton model may have symbolic data
--   variables with guards, assignments, clocks for timing, etc.
data AutSyntax (m :: Type -> Type) loc t tdest
initConf :: AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> m loc
alphabet :: AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> Set t

-- | The transition relation as a function. Note that this function is
--   partial, and only defined for transition labels in the alphabet of the
--   automaton.
trans :: Ord t => AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> loc -> t -> m (tdest, loc)
transRel :: AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> loc -> Map t (m (tdest, loc))

-- | Construct an automaton from an initial state configuration and a
--   transition mapping
automaton :: (BoundedConfiguration m, Completable t, Ord t, Foldable fld) => m loc -> fld t -> (loc -> Map t (m (tdest, loc))) -> AutSyntax m loc t tdest

-- | Construct an automaton from an initial state and a transition mapping
automaton' :: (BoundedMonad m, Completable t, Ord t) => loc -> Set t -> (loc -> Map t (m (tdest, loc))) -> AutSyntax m loc t tdest

-- | Semantical automaton model. This model contains a reference to the
--   syntactical automaton on which it is based, but it also contains a
--   state configuration with semantical states.
--   
--   The difference between the locations from the syntactical model and
--   the states from the semantical model depends on the automaton
--   interpret. E.g. for a simple automaton model, states and locations may
--   be the same, whereas a more complex automaton model may have states
--   consisting of syntactical locations combined with valuations for data
--   variables, clocks for timing, etc.
data AutIntrpr (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act
stateConf :: AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> m q
syntacticAutomaton :: AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> AutSyntax m loc t tdest

-- | Infer a semantical model from a syntactical model, based on the
--   appropriate instance of the <tt>AutomatonSemantics</tt> typeclass.
--   Note that an automaton may be interpreted in multiple ways, so the
--   type checker may need a hint on which semantical automaton is
--   requested. This can be avoided by calling more specific, pre-typed
--   variants of <a>interpret</a> in
--   <a>Lattest.Adapter.StandardAdapters</a>.
interpret :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act. (StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, Ord q) => AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> (loc -> q) -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act

-- | The given model, in the given configuration.
inConfiguration :: AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> m q -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act

-- | The Completable typeclass defines which types can be used as labels on
--   transitions.
class Completable act

-- | Defines the implicit state configuration reached by a given transition
--   label if that label is omitted from a syntactical automaton. E.g. if a
--   state contains no outgoing transition for an output label, that label
--   is often considered to map to the <a>forbidden</a> state
--   configuration.
implicitDestination :: (Completable act, BoundedConfiguration m) => act -> m any

-- | TransitionSemantics expresses that the interpret of a syntactic
--   transition can be expressed in terms of actions. E.g. symbolic
--   transitions with interaction variables that can be expressed in terms
--   of concrete observed values.
class (Ord t, Completable act, TransitionMapping t act, StateSemantics loc q) => TransitionSemantics loc q t tdest act | t act -> tdest

-- | StateSemantics expresses that the interpret of a syntactic location
--   can be expressed in terms of state q. E.g. a location symbolic
--   variables can be given in terms of valuations of these variables.
class StateSemantics loc q

-- | StepSemantics expresses that we can move through an automaton run with
--   state semantics by applying the transition semantics The transition
--   consists of two parts: one global part outside the configuration monad
--   (e.g. describing the action that applies to that transition),
--   described by the transition semantics, and a local part inside the
--   monad, bound to the destination state (e.g. to update symbolic
--   variables for a state).
class (StateSemantics loc q, BoundedMonad m) => StepSemantics (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act

-- | Take a step for the given action, according to the step semantics to
--   move from one state configuration to another. May throw an
--   AutomatonException.
after :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act. (After m loc q t tdest act, Ord (m q), Ord q) => AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act

-- | Take a sequence of transitions for the given actions.
afters :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act. (StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, TransitionSemantics loc q t tdest act, Ord q, Ord (m q)) => AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> [act] -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act
type After (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act = (StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, TransitionSemantics loc q t tdest act, Ord t)
class IOAfter (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act ioState
ioAfter :: IOAfter m loc q t tdest act ioState => ioState -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> IO (AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act)

-- | Exceptions that can occur when working with automata.
newtype AutomatonException

-- | Thrown during a computation of <a>after</a> for an action outside the
--   automaton alphabet.
ActionOutsideAlphabet :: CallStack -> AutomatonException

-- | The class of automata with a finite list of concrete actions matching
--   to outgoing transitions for every state. This property is useful for
--   e.g. test selection.
class TransitionMapping t act => FiniteMenu t act

-- | Menu of specified actions that are semantically present in the
--   automaton.
specifiedMenu :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act. (StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, TransitionSemantics loc q t tdest act, Foldable m, Ord act, Ord q, Ord (m q), FiniteMenu t act) => AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> [act]
data IntrpState a
IntrpState :: a -> Valuation -> IntrpState a
data Valuation
newtype STStdest
STSLoc :: (SymGuard, VarModel) -> STStdest
stsTLoc :: SymGuard -> VarModel -> STStdest

-- | Compute the set of locations that is syntactically reachable from the
--   initial location configuration. See <a>reachableFrom</a>.
reachable :: forall loc (m :: Type -> Type) t tdest. (Ord loc, Foldable m) => AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> Set loc

-- | Compute the set of locations that is syntactically reachable from the
--   given locations.
--   
--   Note that this not involve any interpretation of the automaton, e.g.
--   if a location of symbolic automaton is only reachable via a transition
--   with a guard that is always <a>False</a>, then that location is still
--   considered to be reachable, even if a symbolic interpretation of that
--   automaton can never reach that location for any trace of concrete
--   values.
reachableFrom :: forall loc (m :: Type -> Type) f t tdest. (Ord loc, Foldable m, Foldable f) => AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> f loc -> Set loc
prettyPrint :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) tdest loc t. (Show (m (tdest, loc)), Show (m loc), Show loc, Show t, Ord loc, Foldable m) => AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> String
prettyPrintFrom :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) tdest loc t f. (Show (m (tdest, loc)), Show (m loc), Show loc, Show t, Ord loc, Foldable m, Foldable f) => AutSyntax m loc t tdest -> f loc -> String
prettyPrintIntrp :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) tdest loc q t act. (Show (m (tdest, loc)), Show (m loc), Show loc, Show (m q), Show t, Ord loc, Foldable m) => AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> String
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOGateValue i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSymInteract i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable act
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Automaton.AutomatonException
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest
instance GHC.Exception.Type.Exception Lattest.Model.Automaton.AutomatonException
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.FiniteMenu (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSuspAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.FiniteMenu (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IFAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.FiniteMenu (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SuspendedIF i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.FiniteMenu act act
instance (Lattest.Model.Automaton.StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, Lattest.Model.Automaton.IOTransitionSemantics loc q t tdest act z, Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BooleanConfiguration m, Data.Foldable.Foldable m, GHC.Classes.Ord q, GHC.Classes.Ord t, GHC.Classes.Ord (m q)) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.IOAfter m loc q t tdest act (GHC.IORef.IORef z)
instance (Lattest.Model.Automaton.After m loc q t tdest act, GHC.Classes.Ord (m q), GHC.Classes.Ord q) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.IOAfter m loc q t tdest act ()
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.IOTransitionSemantics loc (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState loc) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSymInteract i o) Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSuspGateValue i o) Lattest.SMT.SMTData.SmtEnv
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.IOTransitionSemantics loc (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState loc) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSymInteract i o) Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IFGateValue i o) Lattest.SMT.SMTData.SmtEnv
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.IOTransitionSemantics loc (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState loc) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSymInteract i o) Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SuspendedIFGateValue i o) Lattest.SMT.SMTData.SmtEnv
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Automaton.AutomatonException
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Automaton.AutomatonException
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState a)
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.StateSemantics a (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState a)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.StateSemantics q q
instance (Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g'), Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BoundedMonad m) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.StepSemantics m loc (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState loc) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract g) Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g')
instance (Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionSemantics q q t () act, Lattest.Model.BoundedMonad.BoundedMonad m) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.StepSemantics m q q t () act
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSuspAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IFAct i o)
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SuspendedIF i o)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord g, Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping g g') => Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract g) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g')
instance Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping act act
instance (Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g'), GHC.Classes.Ord g, Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionMapping g g') => Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionSemantics loc (Lattest.Model.Automaton.IntrpState loc) (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SymInteract g) Lattest.Model.Automaton.STStdest (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.GateValue g')
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionSemantics q q (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) () (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOSuspAct i o)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionSemantics q q (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) () (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IFAct i o)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord i, GHC.Classes.Ord o) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionSemantics q q (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.IOAct i o) () (Lattest.Model.Alphabet.SuspendedIF i o)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord act, Lattest.Model.Automaton.Completable act) => Lattest.Model.Automaton.TransitionSemantics q q act () act


-- | This module contains some simple automata types, and auxiliary
--   functions for constructing them in a convenient manner. Note that the
--   types are no more than type aliases, so if the automata type with your
--   favorite combination of type parameters is not listed, don't hesitate
--   to construct an automaton type (<a>AutSyntax</a> or <a>AutIntrpr</a>)
--   yourself instead of using the StandardAutomata. The base function
--   <a>automaton</a> for constructing automata is re-exported here for
--   convenience.
module Lattest.Model.StandardAutomata

-- | Construct an automaton from an initial state configuration and a
--   transition mapping
automaton :: (BoundedConfiguration m, Completable t, Ord t, Foldable fld) => m loc -> fld t -> (loc -> Map t (m (tdest, loc))) -> AutSyntax m loc t tdest

-- | construct an alphabet of input-output-actions (<a>IOAct</a>) from
--   separate alphabets of inputs and outputs
ioAlphabet :: (Traversable t, Ord i, Ord o) => t i -> t o -> Set (IOAct i o)

-- | Create a deterministic concrete transition relation from an explicit
--   list of tuples, with the destination of transitions expressed as
--   explicit states. Having multiple occurrences of a transition label is
--   forbidden, i.e., leads to a Nothing result.
detConcTransFromRel :: (Ord loc, Ord t) => [(loc, t, loc)] -> Maybe (loc -> Map t (Det ((), loc)))

-- | Create a deterministic concrete transition relation from an explicit
--   list of tuples, with the destination of transitions expressed as
--   deterministic state configuration. Having multiple occurrences of a
--   transition label is forbidden, i.e., leads to a Nothing result.
detConcTransFromMRel :: (Ord loc, Ord t) => [(loc, t, Det loc)] -> Maybe (loc -> Map t (Det ((), loc)))

-- | Create a deterministic concrete transition relation from an explicit
--   list of tuples, with the destination of transitions expressed as Just
--   deterministic states, where <a>Nothing</a> is mapped to either
--   <tt>forbidden</tt> or <tt>underspecified</tt>, depending on the
--   transition label. Having multiple occurrences of a transition label is
--   forbidden, i.e., leads to a Nothing result.
detConcTransFromMaybeRel :: (Completable t, Ord loc, Ord t) => [(loc, t, Maybe loc)] -> Maybe (loc -> Map t (Det ((), loc)))

-- | Create a non-deterministic concrete transition relation from an
--   explicit list of tuples, with the destination of transitions expressed
--   as explicit states. The state configuration must support
--   non-determinism, and having multiple occurrences of a transition label
--   is interpreted as non-deterministic choice between the destinations.
nonDetConcTransFromRel :: (Ord loc, Ord t, OrdMonad m, JoinSemiLattice (m ((), loc))) => [(loc, t, loc)] -> loc -> Map t (m ((), loc))

-- | Create a non-deterministic symbolic transition relation from an
--   explicit list of tuples, with the destination of transitions expressed
--   as explicit states. The state configuration must support
--   non-determinism, and having multiple occurrences of a transition label
--   is interpreted as non-deterministic choice between the destinations.
--   
--   Create a concrete transition relation from an explicit list of tuples,
--   with the destination of transitions expressed as non-deterministic
--   state configuration. The state configuration must support
--   non-determinism, and having multiple occurrences of a transition label
--   is interpreted as non-deterministic choice between the destinations.
nonDetConcTransFromMRel :: (Ord loc, Ord t, OrdMonad m, JoinSemiLattice (m ((), loc))) => [(loc, t, m loc)] -> loc -> Map t (m ((), loc))

-- | Create a non-deterministic concrete transition relation from an
--   explicit list of tuples, with the destination of transitions expressed
--   as lists of locations, where the empty list is mapped to either
--   <tt>forbidden</tt> or <tt>underspecified</tt>, depending on the
--   transition label. Having multiple occurrences of a transition label is
--   interpreted as non-deterministic choice between the destinations.
