nonDetConcTransFromListRel :: (Completable t, Ord loc, Ord t) => [(loc, t, [loc])] -> loc -> Map t (NonDet ((), loc))

-- | Free distributive lattice, or a positive boolean formula, i.e., a
--   boolean formula with conjunctions and disjunctions over atomic
--   propositions. The two elements <a>top</a> and <a>bot</a> can be
--   interpreted as true and false.
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: this implementation is functionally correct, but not
--   very efficient when repeatedly applying operators, especially
--   <a>fmap</a> and monadic bind <a>&gt;&gt;=</a>, since no reductions are
--   performed.
data FreeLattice a

-- | A single state embedded in a free distributive lattice.
atom :: a -> FreeLatticeCNF a

-- | The free distributive lattice element ⊤, or true.
top :: FreeLatticeCNF a

-- | The free distributive lattice element ⊥, or false.
bot :: FreeLatticeCNF a
(\/) :: JoinSemiLattice a => a -> a -> a
(/\) :: MeetSemiLattice a => a -> a -> a

-- | Create a transition relation from a transition function. Warning: to
--   use the resulting transition relation in an automaton, the function
--   must be defined for all reachable states, and for all transition
--   labels in the alphabet of the automaton.
transFromFunc :: (Foldable fld, Ord t) => (loc -> t -> m (tdest, loc)) -> fld t -> loc -> Map t (m (tdest, loc))

-- | Create a concrete transition relation from a transition function.
--   Warning: to use the resulting transition relation in an automaton, the
--   function must be defined for all reachable states, and for all
--   transition labels in the alphabet of the automaton.
concTransFromFunc :: (Foldable fld, Functor m, Ord t) => (loc -> t -> m loc) -> fld t -> loc -> Map t (m ((), loc))

-- | Semantics of automata in which syntactical states and actions are
--   directly interpreted as literal, semantical states and actions.
type ConcreteAutIntrpr (m :: Type -> Type) q act = AutIntrpr m q q act () act

-- | Interpret syntactical states and actions directly as literal,
--   semantical states and actions.
interpretConcrete :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) t loc. (BoundedMonad m, Ord t, Ord loc, Completable t) => AutSyntax m loc t () -> ConcreteAutIntrpr m loc t

-- | Semantics of automata in which syntactical states and actions are
--   directly interpreted as literal, semantical states and actions, but
--   with timeouts as possible output observations.
type ConcreteSuspAutIntrpr (m :: Type -> Type) q i o = AutIntrpr m q q IOAct i o () IOSuspAct i o

-- | Interpret syntactical states and actions are directly as literal,
--   semantical states and actions, but with timeouts as possible output
--   observations.
interpretQuiescentConcrete :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) i o loc. (BoundedMonad m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord loc) => AutSyntax m loc (IOAct i o) () -> ConcreteSuspAutIntrpr m loc i o

-- | Semantics of automata in which syntactical states and actions are
--   directly interpreted as literal, semantical states and actions, but
--   with timeouts and input failures as possible observations.
type ConcreteSuspInputAttemptAutIntrpr (m :: Type -> Type) q i o = AutIntrpr m q q IOAct i o () SuspendedIF i o

-- | Interpret syntactical states and actions are directly as literal,
--   semantical states and actions, but with input failures as possible
--   input observations.
interpretInputAttemptConcrete :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) i o loc. (BoundedMonad m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord loc) => AutSyntax m loc (IOAct i o) () -> ConcreteInputAttemptAutIntrpr m loc i o

-- | Interpret syntactical states and actions are directly as literal,
--   semantical states and actions, but with timeouts and input failures as
--   possible observations.
interpretQuiescentInputAttemptConcrete :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) i o loc. (BoundedMonad m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord loc) => AutSyntax m loc (IOAct i o) () -> ConcreteSuspInputAttemptAutIntrpr m loc i o
type STS (m :: Type -> Type) loc g = AutSyntax m loc SymInteract g STStdest
type IOSTS (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o = STS m loc IOAct i o
type STSIntrp (m :: Type -> Type) loc g = AutIntrpr m loc IntrpState loc SymInteract g STStdest GateValue g
type IOSTSIntrp (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o = STSIntrp m loc IOAct i o
accessSequences :: forall loc (m :: Type -> Type) t tdest act. (Ord loc, Foldable m) => AutIntrpr m loc loc t tdest act -> loc -> Map loc [t]
interpretSTS :: forall loc (m :: Type -> Type) g. (Ord loc, BoundedMonad m, Completable (GateValue g)) => STS m loc g -> Valuation -> STSIntrp m loc g
type SuspSTSIntrp (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o = AutIntrpr m loc IntrpState loc IOSymInteract i o STStdest IOSuspGateValue i o
interpretSTSQuiescent :: forall loc (m :: Type -> Type) i o. (Ord loc, BoundedMonad m) => IOSTS m loc i o -> Valuation -> SuspSTSIntrp m loc i o
type SuspInputAttemptSTSIntrp (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o = AutIntrpr m loc IntrpState loc IOSymInteract i o STStdest SuspendedIFGateValue i o
interpretSTSQuiescentInputAttemptConcrete :: forall loc (m :: Type -> Type) i o. (Ord loc, BoundedMonad m) => IOSTS m loc i o -> Valuation -> SuspInputAttemptSTSIntrp m loc i o


-- | Find concrete values to take transitions in STSes, using an SMT
--   solver.
module Lattest.Model.Symbolic.SolveSTS

-- | For the given STS and a subset function, using SMT solving, find a
--   interaction of the STS in that subset for which the guard is true from
--   the current STS state. The interaction is picked uniformly randomly
--   among interactions with satisfied gates, if any. This uses the
--   supplied random generator and returns the new random generator state.
--   The returned gate values for that interaction are not randomized in
--   any way, picking values is left to the SMT solver.
solveRandomInteraction :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) g r loc g'' g'. (OrdMonad m, BooleanConfiguration m, Ord g, Ord (m (Expr Bool)), RandomGen r) => AutIntrpr m loc (IntrpState loc) (SymInteract g) STStdest (GateValue g'') -> (SymInteract g -> Maybe (SymInteract g')) -> r -> SMT (Maybe (GateValue g'), r)

module Lattest.Exec.ADG.Aut
data Aut a b
Aut :: State a b -> Set (State a b) -> Map a (State a b) -> Set b -> Set b -> Aut a b
[initial] :: Aut a b -> State a b
[states] :: Aut a b -> Set (State a b)
[idStateMap] :: Aut a b -> Map a (State a b)
[inputs] :: Aut a b -> Set b
[outputs] :: Aut a b -> Set b
data State a b
State :: a -> Set b -> Set b -> Map b a -> State a b
[sid] :: State a b -> a
[inp] :: State a b -> Set b
[out] :: State a b -> Set b
[trans] :: State a b -> Map b a

-- | A specialised variant of <a>showsPrec</a>, using precedence context
--   zero, and returning an ordinary <a>String</a>.
show :: Show a => a -> String
after :: (Ord a, Ord b) => State a b -> b -> Aut a b -> Maybe (State a b)
computeCompRel :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b, State a b)
enab :: Ord b => State a b -> Set b
outSet :: Ord b => Set (State a b) -> Set b
afterSet :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Set (State a b) -> b -> Aut a b -> Set (State a b)
statesToAut :: (Ord a, Ord b) => State a b -> Set (State a b) -> Aut a b
addDelta :: Ord a => String -> Aut a String -> Aut a String
afterSequence :: (Ord a, Ord b) => State a b -> [b] -> Aut a b -> Maybe (State a b)
inSet :: Ord b => Set (State a b) -> Set b
getAccesSequences :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> [[b]]
adgAutFromAutomaton :: (Ord a, Ord b) => ConcreteSuspAutIntrpr Det a b b -> b -> Maybe (Aut a b)
getTransitionExtendedAccesSequences :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> [[b]]
union :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Aut a b -> Aut a b
constrAut :: (Ord a, Ord b, Show a, Show b) => (a, Set (a, b, a), Set b, Set b) -> Aut a b
printCompRel :: (Show a, Eq a) => Set (State a b, State a b) -> String
getDistingCompPairs :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> [b] -> Int
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Exec.ADG.Aut.State a b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord a, GHC.Classes.Ord b) => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Exec.ADG.Aut.State a b)
instance (GHC.Show.Show a, GHC.Show.Show b) => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Exec.ADG.Aut.Aut a b)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Exec.ADG.Aut.State a b)

module Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph
data SplitGraph a b
data SplitNode a b
buildSplitGraph :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> SplitGraphAdmin -> (SplitGraph a b, SplitGraphAdmin)

-- | A specialised variant of <a>showsPrec</a>, using precedence context
--   zero, and returning an ordinary <a>String</a>.
show :: Show a => a -> String
getLCA :: (Ord a, Ord b) => SplitGraph a b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (SplitNode a b)
getLCAgreedy :: (Ord a, Ord b) => SplitGraph a b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (SplitNode a b)
getAllLCAsTopDown :: (Ord a, Ord b) => SplitGraph a b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (SplitNode a b)
evidence :: SplitNode a b -> Maybe (Evidence b)
injectiveSet :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> Set b -> Bool
injectiveSetWithInfo :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> Set b -> (Bool, Set (Set (State a b)))
data Evidence b
Nil :: Evidence b
Prefix :: b -> Evidence b -> Evidence b
Plus :: [Evidence b] -> Evidence b
size :: SplitGraph a b -> Int
isUnsplittable :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Set (State a b) -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> Bool
depth :: Ord b => Evidence b -> Int
initializeSplitGraphAdmin :: Bool -> Bool -> Bool -> SplitGraphAdmin
data SplitGraphAdmin
splitGraphAdminToString :: SplitGraphAdmin -> String
getNodeSizeCount :: SplitGraph a b -> Map Int Int
getRootNode :: (Ord a, Ord b) => SplitGraph a b -> SplitNode a b
getMaxInjective :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> (t a b -> SplitNode a b) -> Set (t a b) -> t a b
evidenceInjectiveForStates :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Evidence b -> Set (State a b) -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> (Bool, Set (Set (State a b)))
getObservations :: Ord b => Evidence b -> Set [b]
getWeight :: (Ord a, Ord b, Show b) => [b] -> Evidence b -> Set (State a b) -> Aut a b -> Int
instance (GHC.Classes.Eq b, GHC.Classes.Eq a) => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.ATup a b)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq b => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.Evidence b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Eq a, GHC.Classes.Eq b) => GHC.Classes.Eq (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.SplitNode a b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord b, GHC.Classes.Ord a) => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.ATup a b)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord b => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.Evidence b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord a, GHC.Classes.Ord b) => GHC.Classes.Ord (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.SplitNode a b)
instance GHC.Show.Show b => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.Evidence b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord a, GHC.Classes.Ord b, GHC.Show.Show a, GHC.Show.Show b) => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.SplitGraph a b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord a, GHC.Classes.Ord b, GHC.Show.Show a, GHC.Show.Show b) => GHC.Show.Show (Lattest.Exec.ADG.SplitGraph.SplitNode a b)

module Lattest.Exec.ADG.DistGraph
getPairsForState :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Set (State a b, State a b) -> State a b -> Set (Set (State a b))
getStartStatesLeaves :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Evidence b -> [Set (State a b)]
getEvidenceStats :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Evidence b -> (Int, Int, Int, Int, Int, Int)
computeAdaptiveDistGraph :: (Ord a, Ord b) => Aut a b -> Bool -> Bool -> Bool -> Evidence b

module System.IO.Streams.Synchronized
data TInputStream a
read :: TInputStream a -> STM (Maybe a)
readAll :: TInputStream a -> STM [Maybe a]
unRead :: a -> TInputStream a -> STM ()
hasInput :: TInputStream a -> STM Bool
fromInputStreamBuffered :: InputStream a -> IO (TInputStream a)
makeTInputStream :: STM (Maybe a) -> STM Bool -> IO (TInputStream a)
fromBuffer :: TQueue (Maybe a) -> IO (TInputStream a)
fromTMVar :: TMVar (Maybe a) -> IO (TInputStream a)
duplicate :: OutputStream a -> OutputStream a -> IO (OutputStream a)
map :: (a -> b) -> TInputStream a -> IO (TInputStream b)
mapUnbuffered :: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> TInputStream a -> TInputStream b
mergeBuffered :: TInputStream a -> TInputStream a -> IO (TInputStream a)
mergeBufferedWith :: (TInputStream a -> TInputStream a -> STM [Maybe a]) -> TInputStream a -> TInputStream a -> IO (TInputStream a)
consumeBufferedWith :: STM [Maybe a] -> STM Bool -> IO (TInputStream a)
data Streamed a
Available :: a -> Streamed a
Unavailable :: Streamed a
StreamClosed :: Streamed a
tryRead :: TInputStream a -> STM (Streamed a)
tryReadIO :: TInputStream a -> IO (Streamed a)
tryReadIO' :: TInputStream a -> IO (Maybe a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Streamed a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.TInputStreamClosedException
instance GHC.Exception.Type.Exception System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.TInputStreamClosedException
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Streamed a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.TInputStreamClosedException
instance GHC.Read.Read a => GHC.Read.Read (System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Streamed a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Streamed a)
instance GHC.Show.Show System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.TInputStreamClosedException

module System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Attoparsec
parseFromStream :: TVar (Bool, IResult ByteString (Maybe r)) -> Parser ByteString (Maybe r) -> TInputStream ByteString -> STM (Maybe r)
parserToInputStream :: Parser (Maybe r) -> TInputStream ByteString -> IO (TInputStream r)
instance GHC.Exception.Type.Exception System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Attoparsec.ParseException
instance GHC.Show.Show System.IO.Streams.Synchronized.Attoparsec.ParseException

module Lattest.Adapter.Adapter

-- | An adapter to a (usually external) system. Uses two channels for
--   interaction: one to send input commands, and one to receive outputs.
data Adapter act i
Adapter :: OutputStream i -> TInputStream act -> IO () -> Adapter act i

-- | Channel for sending input commands.
[inputCommandsToSut] :: Adapter act i -> OutputStream i

-- | Channel for receiving outputs.
[actionsFromSut] :: Adapter act i -> TInputStream act

-- | Close the (channels of this) adapter.
[close] :: Adapter act i -> IO ()

-- | Send an input to the adapter.
send :: i -> Adapter act i -> IO ()

-- | Observe in a blocking manner, with two possible outcomes:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>An observation is made,</li>
--   <li>The stream has closed, and any further attempts will give the same
--   result.</li>
--   </ul>
observe :: Adapter act i -> IO (Maybe act)

-- | Try to observe, without blocking, with three possible outcomes:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>An observation is made,</li>
--   <li>no observation was ready, so no observation was made but another
--   attempt may make an observation,</li>
--   <li>The stream has closed, and any further attempts will give the same
--   result.</li>
--   </ul>
tryObserve :: Adapter act i -> IO (Streamed act)

-- | Map function over both the inputs sent to, and the outputs received
--   from, the adapter.
map :: (i' -> i) -> (act -> act') -> Adapter act i -> IO (Adapter act' i')

-- | Map a function over the inputs sent to the adapter.
mapTestChoices :: (i' -> i) -> Adapter act i -> IO (Adapter act i')

-- | Map a function over the outputs received from the adapter.
mapActionsFromSut :: (act -> act') -> Adapter act i -> IO (Adapter act' i)

-- | Transform a raw adapter which receives ByteStrings to an adapter which
--   receives objects, parsing the bytestrings with the given parser.
parseActionsFromSut :: Parser act -> Adapter ByteString i -> IO (Adapter act i)


-- | This module contains the main functions and data structures to run
--   experiments against (external) systems, specifically testing
--   experiments.
--   
--   For an experiment, we assume that we have an <a>Adapter</a>, which
--   serves as an interface to send inputs to a system under test, and
--   observe actions shown by that system. The experiment is controlled by
--   an <a>ActionController</a>. Specifically for a testing experiment, we
--   also assume that we have a specification model that dictates which
--   observations are allowed and forbidden, and a test controller that
--   steers the testing.
--   
--   To get started with testing experiments quickly, use the
--   <a>runTester</a> function, and to get the required ingredients, see
--   the following modules:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Lattest.Adapter.StandardAdapters</a> to create adapters,</li>
--   <li><a>Lattest.Exec.StandardTestControllers</a> to create test
--   controllers, and</li>
--   <li><a>Lattest.Model.StandardAutomata</a> to create specification
--   models.</li>
--   </ul>
module Lattest.Exec.Testing

-- | The controller of an experiment.
data ActionController act i r state
ActionController :: state -> (state -> IO (Either (i, state) r)) -> (state -> act -> IO (Either state r)) -> (state -> IO r) -> ActionController act i r state

-- | Any state that the controller needs for its decision making duties.
[controllerState] :: ActionController act i r state -> state

-- | Select an input and compute the new state, <i>or</i> decide to stop
--   the experiment and return a result.
[select] :: ActionController act i r state -> state -> IO (Either (i, state) r)

-- | Provided an observed action, compute the new state <i>or</i> stop the
--   experiment and return a result.
[update] :: ActionController act i r state -> state -> act -> IO (Either state r)

-- | Handle the end of the action stream, i.e. the other end closing,
--   ending the experiment.
[handleClose] :: ActionController act i r state -> state -> IO r

-- | Run an experiment by interacting with the given adapter, controlled by
--   the given action controller. The experiment stops when the action
--   controller decides to. Returns the result returned by the action
--   controller.
runExperiment :: ActionController act i r state -> Adapter act i -> IO r

-- | The controller of a testing experiment. The tester may return a result
--   at the end of a testing experiment. Note that it does <i>not</i> need
--   to return a verdict <a>Pass</a> or <a>Fail</a>, this verdict is
--   automatically inferred based on the specification model. Results can
--   be used to record additional information that the tester is interested
--   in, depending on the controller implementation. For example, the
--   actions observed during a testing experiment, or whether certain
--   coverage criteria were achieved during the experiment.
data TestController (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state i r
TestController :: state -> (TestChoice i act => state -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> m q -> IO (Either (i, state) r)) -> (state -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> m q -> IO (Either state r)) -> (state -> IO r) -> TestController (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state i r

-- | Any state that the controller needs for its decision making duties.
[testControllerState] :: TestController (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state i r -> state

-- | Select a test based on test controller state, the specification (in
--   its current state), and previous specification configuration. Either
--   select a new controller state and a input, <i>or</i> stop testing and
--   return a result from the controller.
[selectTest] :: TestController (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state i r -> TestChoice i act => state -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> m q -> IO (Either (i, state) r)

-- | Select a test based on test controller state, the specification (in
--   its current state), an observed action, and previous specification
--   configuration. Either select a new controller state, <i>or</i> stop
--   testing and return a result from the controller.
[updateTestController] :: TestController (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state i r -> state -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> m q -> IO (Either state r)

-- | Handle the end of the action stream, i.e. the other end closing,
--   ending the experiment.
[handleTestClose] :: TestController (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state i r -> state -> IO r

-- | From a test controller and a specification model, create an action
--   controller. The test controller is used to decide which inputs are
--   supplied to the system under test, and whether to continue or stop
--   testing. The automaton specification model is used to infer whether
--   observed actions are allowed or not, and to return a verdict in case
--   of forbidden or underspecified observations.
makeTester :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i state r. (After m loc q t tdest act, TestChoice i act, Ord q, Ord (m q)) => AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state i r -> ActionController act i (Verdict, r) (AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act, TestController m loc q t tdest act state i r)
makeSMTTester :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i state r. (IOAfter m loc q t tdest act SMTRef, StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, TestChoice i act) => SMTRef -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state i r -> ActionController act i (Verdict, r) (AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act, TestController m loc q t tdest act state i r)

-- | Running a tester requires:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>a specification model in the form of an automaton, as defined in
--   <a>Lattest.Model.Automaton</a></li>
--   <li>a <a>TestController</a>, defined in this module itself, and</li>
--   <li>an adapter, as defined in <a>Lattest.Adapter.Adapter</a>.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Running a testing experiment is done by interacting with the given
--   adapter, controlled by the given test controller. The experiment stops
--   when the action controller decides to, or when an observation is made
--   that is forbidden or underspecified according to the specification
--   model. Returns the test verdict according to the specification model
--   and the additional result returned by the test controller.
runTester :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i state r. (After m loc q t tdest act, TestChoice i act, Ord q, Ord (m q)) => AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state i r -> Adapter act i -> IO (Verdict, r)
runSMTTester :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i state r. (IOAfter m loc q t tdest act SMTRef, StepSemantics m loc q t tdest act, TestChoice i act) => SMTRef -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state i r -> Adapter act i -> IO (Verdict, r)

-- | The verdict resulting from a testing experiment: did the system under
--   test pass the test?
data Verdict
Pass :: Verdict
Fail :: Verdict
Inconclusive :: InconclusiveReason -> Verdict

-- | In case of an inconclusive verdict, details on why the test is
--   inconclusive
newtype InconclusiveReason
AutomatonException :: AutomatonException -> InconclusiveReason
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Exec.Testing.InconclusiveReason
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Lattest.Exec.Testing.Verdict
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Exec.Testing.InconclusiveReason
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Lattest.Exec.Testing.Verdict
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Exec.Testing.InconclusiveReason
instance GHC.Show.Show Lattest.Exec.Testing.Verdict


-- | This module contains building blocks for constructing out-of-the-box
--   <a>TestController</a>s.
--   
--   <a>TestController</a>s have multiple duties during testing:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>selecting test inputs,</li>
--   <li>deciding whether to continue testing,</li>
--   <li>returning testing results (other than <tt>Pass</tt> or
--   <tt>Fail</tt>), and</li>
--   <li>potentially performing side effects during testing.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   The building blocks in this module allow composing
--   <a>TestController</a>s in a modular way, by combining various choices
--   for these responsibilities. Every building block carries its own
--   state. For example, to stop testing after a fixed number of steps, a
--   state in the form of a counter is needed, whereas returning the
--   observed trace as test result requires recording the observed trace as
--   state.
module Lattest.Exec.StandardTestControllers

-- | Create a <a>TestSelector</a>. Requires one function to select an
--   input, also updating the state, and one function to update the state
--   when observing an action.
selector :: state -> (state -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> m q -> IO (Maybe (i, state))) -> (state -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> m q -> IO (Maybe state)) -> TestSelector m loc q t tdest act state i

-- | <tt>Testselector</tt>s are test controllers that are only concerned
--   with selecting inputs for testing. They do not return any testing
--   results.
type TestSelector (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act s i = TestController m loc q t tdest act s i ()

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks inputs uniformly pseudo-randomly from
--   the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration.
randomTestSelector :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i. (After m loc q t tdest act, FiniteMenu t act, Foldable m, TestChoice i act, Ord act, Ord q, Ord (m q)) => IO (TestSelector m loc q t tdest act StdGen i)

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks inputs uniformly pseudo-randomly from
--   the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration,
--   starting with the given random seed.
randomTestSelectorFromSeed :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i. (After m loc q t tdest act, FiniteMenu t act, Foldable m, TestChoice i act, Ord act, Ord q, Ord (m q)) => Int -> TestSelector m loc q t tdest act StdGen i

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks inputs uniformly pseudo-randomly from
--   the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration, based
--   on the given random generator.
randomTestSelectorFromGen :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act i g. (After m loc q t tdest act, FiniteMenu t act, Foldable m, TestChoice i act, RandomGen g, Ord act, Ord q, Ord (m q)) => g -> TestSelector m loc q t tdest act g i

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks inputs uniformly pseudo-randomly from
--   the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration.
randomDataTestSelector :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o. (StepSemantics m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOGateValue i o), BooleanConfiguration m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord (m SymGuard)) => SMTRef -> IO (TestSelector m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOGateValue i o) (StdGen, SMTRef) (GateValue i))

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks inputs uniformly pseudo-randomly from
--   the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration,
--   starting with the given random seed.
randomDataTestSelectorFromSeed :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o. (StepSemantics m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOGateValue i o), BooleanConfiguration m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord (m SymGuard)) => SMTRef -> Int -> TestSelector m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOGateValue i o) (StdGen, SMTRef) (GateValue i)

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks input gates uniformly pseudo-randomly
--   from the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration,
--   based on the given random generator, with arbitrary data values as
--   picked by the given SMT solver. Will immediately stop if it cannot
--   find any possible data values for any input gate.
randomDataTestSelectorFromGen :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o g. (StepSemantics m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOGateValue i o), BooleanConfiguration m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord (m SymGuard), RandomGen g) => SMTRef -> g -> TestSelector m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOGateValue i o) (g, SMTRef) (GateValue i)

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks input gates uniformly pseudo-randomly
--   from the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration,
--   with arbitrary data values as picked by the given SMT solver. See
--   <a>randomDataOrWaitForOutputTestSelectorFromGen</a> for details.
randomDataOrWaitForOutputTestSelector :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o i'. (StepSemantics m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOSuspGateValue i' o), BooleanConfiguration m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord (m SymGuard)) => SMTRef -> Double -> IO (TestSelector m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOSuspGateValue i' o) (StdGen, SMTRef) (Maybe (GateValue i)))

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks input gates uniformly pseudo-randomly
--   from the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration,
--   starting with the given random seed., with arbitrary data values as
--   picked by the given SMT solver. See
--   <a>randomDataOrWaitForOutputTestSelectorFromGen</a> for details.
randomDataOrWaitForOutputTestSelectorFromSeed :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o i'. (StepSemantics m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOSuspGateValue i' o), BooleanConfiguration m, Ord i, Ord o, Ord (m SymGuard)) => SMTRef -> Int -> Double -> TestSelector m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOSuspGateValue i' o) (StdGen, SMTRef) (Maybe (GateValue i))

-- | A <a>TestSelector</a> that picks input gates uniformly pseudo-randomly
--   from the outgoing transitions from the current state configuration,
--   based on the given random generator, with arbitrary data values as
--   picked by the given SMT solver. It may instead also omit picking an
--   input gate, and wait for an output value instead, with the givene
--   probability (clamped to [0,1]). Will always wait for an output if it
--   cannot find any possible data values for any input gate.
randomDataOrWaitForOutputTestSelectorFromGen :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc i o i' g. (StepSemantics m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOSuspGateValue i' o), BooleanConfiguration m, Ord i, Ord o, RandomGen g, Ord (m SymGuard)) => SMTRef -> g -> Double -> TestSelector m loc (IntrpState loc) (IOSymInteract i o) STStdest (IOSuspGateValue i' o) (g, SMTRef) (Maybe (GateValue i))

-- | Creates a TestController that first selects inputs according to the
--   first provided TestController, and when that first TestController is
--   finished, selects inputs according to the second provided
--   TestController
--   
--   Note: the result from the first tester is currently dropped.
andThen :: forall i act (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest state1 r1 state2 r2. TestChoice i act => TestController m loc q t tdest act state1 i r1 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state2 i r2 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act (Either state1 state2) i r2

-- | <a>StopCondition</a>s are test controllers that are only concerned
--   with deciding whether to continue testing after observing an action.
--   They do not select inputs or return any testing results.
type StopCondition (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act s = TestController m loc q t tdest act s () ()

-- | Create a state-based stop condition, starting in the given initial
--   state. The provided function should provide either <a>Just</a> a new
--   state to continue testing, or <a>Nothing</a> to stop testing.
stopCondition :: s -> (s -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> m q -> IO (Maybe s)) -> StopCondition m loc q t tdest act s

-- | Apply a stop condition: run the (first) test controller until it
--   returns a result, or until the stop condition is reached. The selected
--   inputs and returned result come from the test controller.
untilCondition :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 state2 i2 r2. TestController m loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state2 i2 r2 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act (state1, state2) i1 r1

-- | Observe a fixed number of actions, and then stop testing.
--   
--   Note: since stop conditions only decide whether to stop testing after
--   observing an action, the minimum number of actions to observe is 1.
stopAfterSteps :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act. Int -> StopCondition m loc q t tdest act Int

-- | <a>TestObserver</a>s are only concerned with returning a result after
--   testing. They do not select inputs or decide whether to continue
--   testing.
type TestObserver (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act s r = TestController m loc q t tdest act s () r

-- | Create a <a>TestObserver</a>.
observer :: s -> (s -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> m q -> IO s) -> (s -> IO r) -> TestObserver m loc q t tdest act s r

-- | Apply an observer: Use the (former) test controller, but also
--   returning the result of the (latter) observer. Combine the two results
--   using the given function.
andObservingWith :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 r2 r state2 i2. TestController m loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 -> (r1 -> r2 -> r) -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state2 i2 r2 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act (state1, state2) i1 r

-- | Apply an observer: use the (former) test controller, but also
--   returning the result of the (latter) observer in a tuple.
andObserving :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 state2 i2 r2. TestController m loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state2 i2 r2 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act (state1, state2) i1 (r1, r2)

-- | Apply an observer: use the (former) test controller, but only return
--   the result of the (latter) observer.
observingOnly :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 state2 i2 r2. TestController m loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state2 i2 r2 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act (state1, state2) i1 r2

-- | A <a>TestObserver</a> that returns the trace of all observed actions
traceObserver :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act. TestObserver m loc q t tdest act [act] [act]

-- | A <a>TestObserver</a> that returns the last state configuration of the
--   specification model. This may be a conclusive state. For example,
--   during a failed test, this observer returns <tt>forbidden</tt>.
stateObserver :: TestObserver m loc q t tdest act (Maybe (m q)) (Maybe (m q))

-- | A <a>TestObserver</a> that returns the last inconclusive state
--   configuration of the specification model. For example, during a
--   failing test, this observer returns the last state before the failure.
inconclusiveStateObserver :: BoundedConfiguration m => TestObserver m loc q t tdest act (Maybe (m q)) (Maybe (m q))

-- | <a>TestSideEffect</a>s perform side effects during testing, but have
--   no impact on the testing itself, nor on the result.
type TestSideEffect (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act s = TestController m loc q t tdest act s () ()

-- | Apply a side effect: Use the (former) test controller, but also
--   perform the side effect of the (latter) observer.
withSideEffect :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 state2 i2 r2. TestController m loc q t tdest act state1 i1 r1 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act state2 i2 r2 -> TestController m loc q t tdest act (state1, state2) i1 r1

-- | Create a <a>TestSideEffect</a>. The provided function returns the new
--   state in an <a>IO</a> monad that can also perform the side effects.
testSideEffect :: s -> (s -> AutIntrpr m loc q t tdest act -> act -> m q -> IO s) -> TestSideEffect m loc q t tdest act s

-- | Print observed to stdin actions during testing.
printActions :: forall act (m :: Type -> Type) loc q t tdest. Show act => TestSideEffect m loc q t tdest act ()

-- | Print the state configuration of the specification during testing.
printState :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) q loc t tdest act. Show (m q) => TestSideEffect m loc q t tdest act ()


-- | This module contains building blocks for constructing out-of-the-box
--   <a>Adapter</a>s.
--   
--   Note that adapters do not observe inputs by default. In other words,
--   an input command from a test controller does not automatically lead to
--   an input transition in the specification model. Use
--   <a>acceptingInputs</a> to observe all sent inputs, or
--   <a>acceptingInputsWithIncompletenessAsFailures</a> to also observe
--   failures in processing inputs in the adapter. The latter is useful if
--   the processing of inputs is defined partially, in which case some
--   inputs cannot be sent to the adapter.
module Lattest.Adapter.StandardAdapters

-- | An adapter to a (usually external) system. Uses two channels for
--   interaction: one to send input commands, and one to receive outputs.
data Adapter act i

-- | adapter which, after every input, directly sends the corresponding
--   sequence of outputs
pureMealyAdapter :: (state -> i -> state) -> (state -> i -> [act]) -> state -> IO (Adapter act i)

-- | Adapter which, after every action, has the given probability of
--   producing non-deterministically one of the corresponding (non-timeout)
--   output transitions if any is available. After receiving a Nothing
--   input, an output will be produced, which may be a timeout.
pureAdapter :: (Ord i, Ord o, RandomGen g) => g -> Double -> (state -> Map (IOAct i o) state) -> state -> IO (Adapter (SuspendedIF i o) (Maybe i))

-- | Settings for a socket Adapter.
data SocketSettings act i
SocketSettings :: HostName -> PortNumber -> SocketSettings act i
[hostName] :: SocketSettings act i -> HostName
[portNumber] :: SocketSettings act i -> PortNumber

-- | Default settings for a socket Adapter.
baseSocketSettings :: SocketSettings act i

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the default
--   settings.
connectSocketAdapter :: IO (Adapter ByteString ByteString)

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the given
--   settings.
connectSocketAdapterWith :: SocketSettings act i -> IO (Adapter ByteString ByteString)

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the default
--   settings, and sending inputs and reading outputs in JSON format.
connectJSONSocketAdapter :: (ToJSON i, FromJSON o) => IO (Adapter o i)

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the given
--   settings, and sending inputs and reading outputs in JSON format.
connectJSONSocketAdapterWith :: (ToJSON i, FromJSON o) => SocketSettings act i -> IO (Adapter o i)

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the default
--   settings, and sending inputs and reading outputs in JSON format,
--   observing any input as accepted.
connectJSONSocketAdapterAcceptingInputs :: (ToJSON i, FromJSON o) => IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i)

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the given
--   settings, and sending inputs and reading outputs in JSON format,
--   observing any input as accepted.
connectJSONSocketAdapterAcceptingInputsWith :: (ToJSON i, FromJSON o) => SocketSettings act i -> IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i)

-- | Take an adapter that sends raw <a>ByteString</a>s, and transform it to
--   an adapter that sends <a>String</a>s encoded in utf-8.
encodeUtf8 :: Adapter act ByteString -> IO (Adapter act String)

-- | Take an adapter that receives raw <a>ByteString</a>s, and transform it
--   to an adapter that receives <a>String</a>s decoded from utf-8.
decodeUtf8 :: Adapter ByteString i -> IO (Adapter String i)

-- | Take an adapter that sends and receives raw <a>ByteString</a>s, and
--   transform it to an adapter that sends and receives <a>String</a>s,
--   encoded in utf-8 and decoded from utf-8.
endecodeUtf8 :: Adapter ByteString ByteString -> IO (Adapter String String)

-- | Take an adapter that sends raw <a>ByteString</a>s, and transform it to
--   an adapter that sends any type encoded in JSON.
encodeJSONTestChoices :: ToJSON i => Adapter act ByteString -> IO (Adapter act i)

-- | Take an adapter that receives raw <a>ByteString</a>s, and transform it
--   to an adapter that receives any type decoded from JSON.
parseJSONActionsFromSut :: FromJSON act => Adapter ByteString i -> IO (Adapter act i)

-- | Transform a given Adapter to accept all inputs. In other words, every
--   selected input command directly becomes an observed input action.
--   Every observed output from the given Adapter becomes an output action.
acceptingInputs :: Adapter o i -> IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i)

-- | Transform a given Adapter to accept all inputs, but if the given
--   Adapter would call an undefined case in a partial function, then
--   interpret this as an observed input failure. Any input command that
--   does not call undefined cases of partial functions directly become
--   observed input action. Every observed output from the given Adapter
--   becomes an output action.
--   
--   Input failures are useful for Adapters written in pure haskell.
--   Instead of only writing complete functions to process input commands,
--   where unexpected input commands may e.g. lead to an error output, the
--   adapter may also just process input commands with partial functions
--   instead, avoiding some boilerplate code.
acceptingInputsWithIncompletenessAsFailures :: Adapter o i -> IO (Adapter (IFAct i o) i)

-- | Transform the given Adapter by introducing quiescence (timeout
--   observations). Inputs can be sent as <a>Just</a> inputs, and
--   <a>IOAct</a> observations can be done as in the provided adapter,
--   where the outputs in the <a>IOAct</a> are wrapped in <tt>SuspAct</tt>
--   to denote that they are real outputs. Additionally:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>An artificial <a>Quiescence</a> output observation is made after
--   the provided timeout value. This timeout is measured since the last
--   <a>Just</a> input or <tt>SuspOut</tt> output has been received.</li>
--   <li>An artificial <a>Nothing</a> input can be made, which indicates
--   waiting for an output. Sending a <a>Nothing</a> will block until any
--   output is received, which may be a real <tt>SuspAct</tt> output or
--   <a>Quiescence</a>. This is guaranteed to happen within the given
--   timeout value, give or take a few milliseconds for processing.</li>
--   </ul>
withQuiescence :: NominalDiffTime -> Adapter (IOAct i o) i -> IO (Adapter (IOSuspAct i o) (Maybe i))

-- | Transform the given Adapter by introducing quiescence (timeout
--   observations). See <a>withQuiescence</a>, where the waiting time given
--   in milliseconds.
withQuiescenceMillis :: Int -> Adapter (IOAct i o) i -> IO (Adapter (IOSuspAct i o) (Maybe i))

-- | Transform the given Adapter by introducing a short delay before
--   passing on any provided input. If, during that delay, an observation
--   is made, then that input is _not_ passed on.
--   
--   This may be used to slow down a tester which performs inputs too fast
--   for observation responses to occur, both after sending an input and
--   after making an observation.
withInputDelay :: NominalDiffTime -> Adapter (IOAct i o) i' -> IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i')

-- | Transform the given Adapter by introducing a short delay before every
--   provided input. See <a>withInputDelay</a>, with the delay time given
--   in milliseconds.
withInputDelayMillis :: Int -> Adapter (IOAct i o) i' -> IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i')

-- | Transform the given Adapter by introducing a short delay after every
--   provided input. After an input is provided, then observing the adapter
--   (or even calling <tt>hasObservation</tt>) will block until the
--   specified time duration has passed, or until an output is observed, or
--   until the action stream of the adapter closes, whichever comes first.
--   
--   This may be used to slow down a tester which performs successive
--   inputs too fast for observation responses to occur.
--   
--   More precisely, this may be needed in case of a state with incoming
--   transitions for inputs, and outgoing transitions for <i>both</i>
--   inputs and outputs. Without input delay, the tester may perform one
--   input to enter the state, immediately observe that input, and
--   immediately perform the second input from that state, whereas the
--   wrapped adapter may receive the first input and respond with an
--   output, followed by the second input. The wrapped adapter and the
--   tester then observe different traces of actions.
--   
--   Note: this function does <i>not</i> help to resolve states with an
--   incoming transition for an <i>output</i>, and transitions for both
--   inputs and output. To resolve that situation, use
--   <a>withInputDelay</a> instead.
withSuccessiveInputDelay :: NominalDiffTime -> Adapter (IOAct i o) i' -> IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i')

-- | Transform the given Adapter by introducing a short delay after every
--   provided input. See <a>withSuccessiveInputDelay</a>, with the delay
--   time given in milliseconds.
withSuccessiveInputDelayMillis :: Int -> Adapter (IOAct i o) i' -> IO (Adapter (IOAct i o) i')

-- | Transform the given I/O-Adapter (for action <a>IOAct</a> by
--   interpreting the input and output actions as gate values with data
--   parameters.
asSymbolicSuspAdapter :: Adapter (IOSuspAct (GateValue i) (GateValue o)) (Maybe (GateValue i)) -> IO (Adapter (IOSuspGateValue i o) (Maybe (GateValue i)))

-- | Create an adapter by connecting to a server socket, with the default
--   settings, and sending inputs and reading outputs with data, in JSON
--   format, observing any input as accepted.
connectJSONSocketAdapterSTSwithQuiescence :: (ToJSON i, FromJSON o) => Int -> IO (Adapter (IOSuspGateValue i o) (Maybe (GateValue i)))

module Lattest.Exec.StandardTestControllers.CompleteTestSuite

-- | A TestController that selects inputs that lead to the given
--   targetState. If unexpected outputs are selected by the SUT the
--   TestSelector still tries to provide the inputs of the access sequence,
--   but this may result in reaching another state. Result Bool is True
--   when access sequence has been followed and false when the SUT deviated
accessSeqSelector :: (Ord q, Eq i, Eq o) => ConcreteSuspAutIntrpr Det q i o -> q -> TestController Det q q (IOAct i o) () (IOSuspAct i o) [IOAct i o] (Maybe i) Bool

-- | A TestController that selects inputs according to the adaptive
--   distinguishing sequence of the given automaton
adgTestSelector :: (Ord q, Ord l) => ConcreteSuspAutIntrpr Det q l l -> l -> TestController Det q q (IOAct l l) () (IOSuspAct l l) (Evidence l) (Maybe l) (Set q)

-- | A TestController that yields tests that tries to take an access
--   sequence to the targetState and then executes the adaptive
--   distinguishing sequence
nCompleteSingleState :: (Ord q, Ord l) => ConcreteSuspAutIntrpr Det q l l -> Int -> Int -> l -> q -> TestController Det q q (IOAct l l) () (IOSuspAct l l) (((), [IOSuspAct l l]), Maybe (Det q)) i20 ([IOSuspAct l l], Maybe (Det q)) -> IO (TestController Det q q (IOAct l l) () (IOSuspAct l l) (Either (Either [IOAct l l] StdGen, Int) (Evidence l), (((), [IOSuspAct l l]), Maybe (Det q))) (Maybe l) ([IOSuspAct l l], Maybe (Det q)))

-- | Runs tests from nCompleteSingleState for each given targetState and
--   seed
runNCompleteTestSuite :: (Ord q, Ord l, Show q, Show l) => IO (Adapter (IOAct l l) l) -> AutSyntax Det q (IOAct l l) () -> Int -> l -> [(q, Int)] -> IO [(q, Verdict, ([IOSuspAct l l], Maybe (Det q)))]
